chunk_2	About Us Discover our institution's story and values Institution Overview Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology, Kodakara Established by the Diocese of Irinjalakuda, Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology was founded with the vision of imparting value-based, quality technical education that integrates professional competence with ethical responsibility. The institution is managed by the Irinjalakuda Diocesan Educational Trust, registered in 2001, under the visionary leadership of His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil, the first Bishop of the Diocese. The college was established in 2002 with approval from the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) and was initially affiliated to the University of Calicut. Located on a picturesque 25-acre green campus at Kodakara, Thrissur District, the institution provides a serene and intellectually stimulating environment conducive to academic excellence, innovation, and holistic development. In alignment with the restructuring of technical education in the State, the institution transitioned in 2015 to affiliation with APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University (APJAKTU), Kerala. Over the years, Sahrdaya has expanded its academic portfolio to offer six undergraduate (B.Tech) programmes:  Biomedical Engineering  Biotechnology Engineering  Civil Engineering  Computer Science and Engineering  Electronics and Communication Engineering  Electrical and Electronics Engineering The institution also offers four postgraduate (M.Tech) programmes:  Biomedical Engineering  Computer Science and Engineering  Industrial Biotechnology  Embedded Systems In addition to undergraduate and postgraduate programmes, the institution offers PhD research programmes in approved disciplines, fostering a strong research culture a nd enabling scholars to pursue advanced studies aligned with contemporary technological and societal needs. The college also offers Postgraduate Diploma programmes in emerging and interdisciplinary areas, aimed at enhancing professional competencies, upskilling graduates, and supporting lifelong learning in response to evolving industry and research demands. In recognition of its academic maturity and governance practices, Academic Autonomy was conferred by the University Grants Commission (UGC) in 2024. This milestone empowered the institution to implement Outcome-Based Education (OBE), the Choice-Based Credit System (CBCS), and flexible, industry-aligned curricula, promoting interdisciplinary learning, innovation, and skill development. Sahrdaya is NAAC-accredited, and all undergraduate engineering programmes are NBA accredited by 2025, reflecting sustained adherence to national quality benchmarks. The institution is well regarded among engineering colleges in Kerala for its disciplined academic environment, consistent student outcomes, and strong value system. The college provides state-of-the-art laboratories, ICT-enabled classrooms, a fully digitized central library, a language laboratory, modern computing facilities, well-maintained hostels, transport services, and comprehensive sports and fitness infrastructure. The lush green campus and eco-friendly initiatives contribute to a vibrant and enriching campus life. Graduates of Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology have progressed to reputed industries, higher education programmes, research institutions, and entrepreneurial ventures, reflecting the institution’s sustained emphasis on academic rigour, professional competence, and lifelong learning. The curriculum design, skill-oriented learning practices, an d industry exposure collectively support students in building successful and meaningful career pathways. Rooted in its founding values, Sahrdaya emphasizes holistic education, nurturing intellectual, physical, emotional, moral, and spiritual growth. Through value education, community service, mentoring, and inclusive campus practices, the institution fosters ethical leadership, social responsibility, compassion, and integrity, while remaining open and welcoming to students from all backgrounds. Aligned with its Vision 2030, Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology aspires to emerge as a centre of excellence in autonomous engineering education, with strengthened focus on research, innovation, entrepreneurship, industry collaboration, global engagement, and sustainable development, shaping future-ready engineers and responsible global citizens.

chunk_6	Campus Amenities Discover our world-class facilities and student amenities Green Campus We proudly recieved “Swatch Campus Award “.The green campus of the College holds a healthy medicinal garden which is a collection of varities of plants having high medicinal values. We can also see plant species which are very rare. Transportation College is providing transportation facilities to various localities. There are total 21 buses for the college. Board Room Excellent A/C Room for Executive meeting Multimedia Conference Room Well equipped A/C Conference Room with a capacity of 150 occupants for executive meetings and video conferences. Snacks Bar We have three snacks bar which are located at bio block, indoor stadium and near to the canteen. Auditorium We have a well-built auditorium with a maximum occupancy of 3000. All major programmes of college are conducted in this auditorium. Fitness Center A student not only want to be mentally strong but also he/she want to be physically fit. There are separate well equipped physical fitness centre for boys and girls. Proper guidance is provided by the fitness trainers .Its open at both morning and evening. Reprographic Cetnter We have two Reprographic centres in the college campus which are located at Bio Block, Decennial block and at Auditorium. Canteen In Sahrdaya we have a clean and hygenic canteen that serves food for students as well as faculty members. We have a proper menu of food which is specific for each day. Fixed time schedule is also provided for food delivery. Hostel Three hostels named Shalome, Jyothis and Thejus are allotted for girls and Santhome hostel for boys. We have a separate PG hostel block too. All the hostels are ragging free and it’s truly a home away from home. Chapel

We also conduct holy rosary everyday and adorations on every Mondays and Thursday ATM In house South Indian Bank ATM, so that Sahrdayans can withdraw can at any convenient time without any hassle.

chunk_20	Management Meet our management team and organizational structure Irinjalakuda Diosesan Educational Trust Irinjalakuda Diocesan Educational Trust was registered on 23-07-2001 with Reg. No: 138/IV 2001. The trust belongs to the Catholic Diocese of Irinjalakuda, Christian (Syro-Malabar) minority community. The Registered Office of the Trust is located at Catholic Bishop’s House, Irinjalakuda – 680121, Manavalassery Village, Mukundapuram Taluk , Thrissur District , Kerala State , India. Patron & Chairman Rt. Rev. Bishop Dr. Pauly Kannookadan Bishop Of Catholic Diocese Of Irinjalakuda Human Behavior Is A Very Broad And Complex Phenomenon, Involving Numerous Components. Each Individual Possesses A Different ‘Package’ Of Experiences, Values, Needs, Goals, Persuasions And Ideas That Cause Him To Behave Differently From Another. The Task Of Education Is To Change The Behavior. For This, Keep Up The Spirit Of Prayer And Hard Work And Remain Well Disciplined. Mutual Trust And Co-Operation Alone Will Help The Institution To Go Ahead With Full Thrust As One Soul And One Heart, Thereby Making The Venture A Reality. Manager Msgr. Wilson Erathara Human mind is a complex unit and all knowledge is interdependent. In a sense, every study should excite and satisfy different mental powers. It must impart intellectual vision, aesthetic enjoyment and practical power to the students. The prime thrust of the diocese is the total transformation of the individual through education by emphasizing the family as the cornerstone of social structure. Intension of our institution is to fabricate each individual as God-fearing and honest citizens, respectful and loving all. Board Members Mar Pauly Kannookadan Msgr. Wilson Erathara Rev. Fr. chunk_26	Profile View detailed institutional profile and achievements Institutional Profile Irinjalakuda Diocese was established in the year 1978, and His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil took over the reins as the first Bishop of this Diocese. Irinjalakuda Diocese is doing a excellent service meeting the needs of Education, Health and Social responsibilities. Considering the demands on the necessity of value based higher technical education, the people of the Diocese decided to establish an Engineering college to mark The Episcopal Silver Jubilee Year of His Excellency Bishop Mar James Pazhayattil and Silver Jubilee of the formation of the Diocese of Irinjalakuda .The Irinjalakuda Diocesan Educational Trust was formed and registered on 23-07-2001 with Reg. No: 138/IV 2001 at Irinjalakuda registration office with His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil as Chairman, Rev. Msgr. Sebastian Ezhekadan as President, Rev. Fr. Joseph Thekkethala as Secretary, Rev.Fr. Pius Chirapanath as Finance Officer. With land holding about 40 acres , the Trust submitted the application form for establishing Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology at Kodakara, to the Government of Kerala, University of Calicut and AICTE , New Delhi through the Regional Office. By the Grace of God, the AICTE, New Delhi in its letter dated 06-06-2002, sanctioned the establishment of Sahrdaya College at Kodakara, to run the four B. Tech Degree courses leading to Electronics and Communication Engineering, Computer Science and Engineering, Biomedical Engineering and Biotechnology (Engineering) with an intake of 60 students in each of the courses from the academic year 2002-2003. The Government of Kerala included the Sahrdaya College as one of the engineering colleges to which the students have to be admitted by the Com
chunk_27	missioner of Entrance Examination from the year 2002-2003 in the four branches sanctioned by the AICTE. On 21st November 2001 His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil laid the foundation stone for central building of Sahrdaya College. Architects Mayphils from Ernakulam prepared the master plan for the college and detail plan for the individual buildings. On /2002 His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil laid the foundation stone for the workshop and hostel building in the campus. In a short span of seven months the central building, workshop and hostel block were completed in all respects. All the 4 class rooms of the first batch were equipped with Networked Computers with LCD projector for Multimedia Presentations, Teak wood furniture, imported green glass Magnetic chalk boards etc. The laboratories required for first year in Physics and Chemistry departments and the workshop required in Mechanical, Electrical & Electronics and Civil engineering departments were fully equipped. A drawing hall containing modern adjustable drawing boards was also made available for use. An advanced Computer Lab with 60 networked Pentium-4 terminals was established. A language lab to train the students in English, Hindi, French, German, Arabic and Japanese languages was also established with necessary audio support from Linguaphone Institute Ltd, London. A vast reference library which can accommodate 150 students at a time and issue library with text books and reference books was established with a well qualified librarian and necessary supporting staff. Library is being digitalized. Well-qualified teaching staff varies from master’s degree to doctorate degree conversant in using power point presentations were recruited. Internet facilities are provided in the college. Buses were provided fo r transporting staff and students. On /2002, the college building was blessed by His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil, Honourable M.L.A.’s viz., Adv. K.P. Viswanathan MLA, Mr. P.P. George MLA, Mr. T.U. Radhakrishnan MLA, Ms. Savithri Lakshmanan, MLA, Mr. M.K. Paulson MLA, Mr. P.R. Prasadan, Village Panchayat President, Rev. Priests, Sisters and Parish Representatives, well wishers and other dignitaries were present on the occasion. The college office started functioning in the new premises with Executive Director, Principal and office staff on 23-09-2002. foundation stone for the workshop and hostel building in the campus. Voltx Workshop 1 month ago "The Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering, in association with the project team SCET VAIDYUTH, is conducting a workshop titled VOLT X from 4th February to 27th February 2026 for S2 students. The workshop aims to provide basic-level technical training related to the development of a solar-powered autonomous electric vehicle. The training will include an introduction to the software tools used in the project, namely MATLAB and TinkerCAD, along with fundamental electrical concepts such as wiring practices, electrical components, types of motors, and their specifications. Students will also be introduced to the mechanical works and integrations involved in the project, giving them an overall understanding of how electrical, software, and mechanical systems are combined in a real-time application. Registration Fee : 30 Rs/- Registration link : https://forms.gle/fGjGobv49h4VWxET9 The faculty in charge for the workshop are Dr. V. Vijikala and Sreekanth S, Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering." chunk_54	Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology (SCET) Celebrating 25 Years of Academic Excellence (Silver Jubilee 2002–2026) Join the Vanguard of Technological Evolution: Nationwide Recruitment “Empowering Minds, Future-Proofing Careers” As Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology enters its Silver Jubilee year , we are embarking on a transformative expansion to redefine the future of engineering education. Along with the current seven departments, we are launching two new departments (AI/ML and Cyber-Physical Systems) and seven pioneering programs , including cutting-edge UG, PG, and 5-year Integrated Master’s degrees. We invite visionary educators, prolific researchers, and industry veterans to join us in our mission to lead innovation, found startups, and ensure the future-proofing of our students. Application Form for Teaching Staff Application Form for Non-Teaching Staff Fill the Application Form Now Hiring: Leadership & Faculty Positions We are seeking dynamic professionals across all departments for the following roles: I. Strategic Leadership Opportunities (Deans) We seek transformative leaders for the following portfolios to drive our institutional vision: Academic Excellence Dean (UG) | Dean (PG) Research & Innovation Dean (Research) | Dean (Consultancy and Industry Connect) Operations & Growth Dean (Administration) | Dean (Planning) Student Success Dean (Student Affairs) | Dean (Career Guidance, Training, and Placement) II. Faculty Positions Senior Professor | Professor | Associate Professor | Assistant Professor Departments & Areas of Specialization Department Areas of Specialization & Research Industry & Consultancy Verticals AI / ML & Computer Science Generative & Agentic AI: LLMs, RAG, Transformers, Few-shot learning. Future Tech: Quantum
chunk_55	ML/Cryptography, Edge Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), Affective Computing. AI Ethics auditing, MLOps, Data Engineering, UI/UX Design, and AI-integrated Full-stack development. Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) Smart Systems: Real-time systems (RTOS), IoT/IIoT, Sensor Fusion, LPWAN (5G/6G), and Digital Twins. Security: Cyber Forensics, Zero-trust Network Security. Industrial Automation (Industry 4.0), Smart City infrastructure, and Secure Network protocols. Electronics Silicon Engineering: Advanced VLSI/ASIC Design, FPGA-based Systems, Signal & Image Processing, Low-power IC Design for Wearables. Hardware Security, SoC (System on Chip) prototyping, and Hardware-level encryption services. Biotechnology Modern Bio-Eng: Computational/Systems Biology, Synthetic Biology, Biopharmaceuticals, Bioprocess Engineering. Bio-process optimization, industrial fermentation, and bio-fuel research. Biomedical Engineering Med-Tech: Biosensor fabrication, Biomechanics, Physiological modelling, Virtual Instrumentation (LabVIEW/Simulink). CAD/Prototyping of medical devices, diagnostic tool design, and regulatory compliance. Electrical & Computer Eng. Energy Systems: EV Charging Infrastructure, Battery Management (BMS), Renewable Energy Integration, AI-driven Control. Microgrid management, Energy Auditing, and Smart Grid stabilization for industry. Civil Resilient Infra: Structural Health Monitoring (SHM), BIM (4D/5D Modeling), Revit architecture, GIS & Remote Sensing. Green Building Certification (LEED), BIM-led Project Management, and Smart Materials. Applied Science & Humanities Foundational Research: Stochastic modeling for AI, Nanomaterials, Ethics in Technology, and Professional Communication. Mathematical modeling for Finance, Material characterization, and Tech-Societal imp act studies. The Sahrdaya Value Proposition: A Path of Growth and Fulfillment A Culture of Innovation : Active involvement in centres of excellence, product development, and industry-academia collaboration. Scholar-Entrepreneur Pathway : Opportunities for funded projects, patent generation, and consultancy. Interdisciplinary Synergy : A collaborative environment where faculty work on collaborative teaching, integrated projects and joint research. Holistic Mentorship : A platform to mentor high-achieving students for global competitions and higher studies. Future-Ready Environment : Access to advanced laboratories and emerging AI technologies. Candidate Profile Qualifications: Ph.D./M.Tech from leading and reputed institutions (IITs, NITs, or equivalent), and industry as per AICTE/UGC norms. Expertise: Proven track record in reputed journals, industry collaboration, and research. Vision: Ability to design curriculum and teach advanced courses in emerging allied areas. How to Apply Join us in redefining the engineering landscape. Through various programs and initiatives, NSS volunteers work towards creating a positive impact in society. We nurture the entrepreneurial spirit and promote innovation-driven initiatives. Learn More PALS Peer Assisted Learning System PALS is a collaborative learning initiative where senior students guide and mentor their juniors. This peer-to-peer learning approach enhances academic performance and builds a strong community of learners. chunk_74	Cells Sahrdaya College operates through various cells dedicated to ensuring quality education, student welfare, and institutional excellence. Explore our cells to learn more about their roles and activities. Internal Complaints Cell ICC Addresses complaints related to sexual harassment and ensures a safe and respectful environment for all members of the college community. Available Information: Constitution Complaint Form View Details Anti-Ragging Cell ARC Works to prevent ragging and ensures strict action against any form of ragging, promoting a healthy and safe campus environment. Available Information: Constitution Activities View Details Anti-Drugs Cell ADC Promotes awareness about substance abuse and works towards creating a drug-free campus through education and intervention programs. Available Information: Constitution Activities View Details Scholarships Cell SC Manages and coordinates various scholarship programs to support deserving students in their academic pursuits. Available Information: Constitution Available Scholarships Scholarship Winners View Details College Union Cell CU Oversees student union activities and facilitates student participation in the governance and cultural life of the institution. Available Information: Constitution Activities View Details Student Grievance Redressal Cell SGRC Provides a platform for students to voice their grievances and ensures timely resolution of academic and non-academic issues. Available Information: Constitution Grievance Form View Details Faculty Grievance Redressal Cell FGRC Addresses concerns and grievances of faculty members, ensuring a fair and conducive working environment. chunk_80	PG Diploma in Data Science and Intelligent Systems The PG Diploma program is designed as an internationally aligned postgraduate qualification integrating advanced coursework, industry-oriented training, and research-based dissertation components. The curriculum is structured to meet global academic standards and to enable academic progression opportunities through collaborations with leading universities in New Zealand. Through this collaborative framework, students may be eligible to transfer selected academic credits (subject to evaluation and approval by the partnering universities) after successful completion of the prescribed modules. This enables students to pursue advanced postgraduate qualifications or Master’s programs abroad by completing the remaining requirements at the partner institution. Students who continue their studies in New Zealand will be awarded degrees by the respective partnering universities upon successful completion of the program requirements. Tuition and admission conditions at the partner institution will be governed by their prevailing regulations. The PG Diploma is a 6-month intensive program structured around advanced core courses, laboratory components, research methodology, industry certification (such as AI-focused workforce training), and dissertation phases. The program is designed to bridge academic excellence with global industry demands. In collaboration with select universities in New Zealand, the program creates opportunities for: Academic articulation pathways Joint supervision for dissertation work Exposure to international research ecosystems Progression into Master’s degree programs Credits earned during the PG Diploma may be considered for adva
chunk_81	nced standing by the partnering universities based on their academic policies. Students opting for international progression will complete the remaining coursework or research components at the partner university and will be awarded the final degree by that institution. The PG Diploma program is designed to foster innovation, applied research, and interdisciplinary expertise. With a structured combination of coursework, laboratory training, research methodology, and dissertation phases, the program prepares students for advanced research and professional careers. Through academic partnerships with universities in New Zealand, the program facilitates: Academic mentoring from international faculty (where applicable) Exposure to global research practices Pathways to postgraduate degree continuation Opportunities for collaborative research initiatives Students completing the diploma may apply for credit recognition or advanced entry into postgraduate programs offered by the partner universities, subject to their admission and credit transfer policies. Final degrees will be conferred by the respective partnering university upon successful completion. This collaborative structure promotes: International academic mobility Global research collaboration Enhanced academic credentials Competitive advantage in higher education and employment sectors Program Structure This program is structured at the Postgraduate Diploma level. Students enrolling in the PG Diploma will complete the prescribed coursework, laboratory components, industry-integrated training, and dissertation phases at the home institution. Upon successful completion of the PG Diploma requirements, eligible students may progress to partnering universities in New Zealand (and other collaborating countries), subject to
chunk_82	admission and credit evaluation policies of the host institution. Students opting for international progression may receive advanced standing or credit recognition, enabling them to complete the remaining academic or research requirements at the host university. Upon successful completion of the prescribed program abroad, the final postgraduate degree will be awarded by the respective partnering university. This structure provides a seamless academic pathway from a PG Diploma to an international Master’s qualification, enhancing global exposure, research opportunities, and international career prospects. Benefits • The program is strengthened through academic collaboration with international partner universities, ensuring exposure to contemporary curricular developments, emerging technologies, and global best practices. • By completing the PG Diploma at the home institution before progressing abroad, students substantially reduce the overall cost of international education compared to pursuing a full overseas program. • Graduates who continue their studies abroad gain access to international job markets with competitive salary packages, enabling faster recovery of educational investment. • Students may gain access to a broader spectrum of advanced electives and specialization areas at partner universities, including niche domains not widely available in India. • Studying across two academic systems fosters cross-cultural competence, adaptability, and global professional readiness. • The program provides a smooth transition to Master’s degree programs at the partner university or other leading global institutions, with potential eligibility for Teaching Assistantships (TA) or Research Assistantships (RA), thereby reducing the financial burden of higher studies. Partnerin g Universities Wintec (Waikato Institute of Technology) NewZealand 6 months @ Sahrdaya+ 1 year @ New Zealand Branch: CSE, IT Award of Degrees: Master of Applied Information Technology, WINTEC Wellington Institute of Technology (WelTec) NewZealand 6 months @ Sahrdaya+ 1 year @ New Zealand Branch: CSE, IT Award of Degrees: Master of Information Technology, WelTec Admission Procedure Step 1: Apply for PG Diploma at Sahrdaya Candidates must first apply for admission to the 6-Month Full-Time PG Diploma in Data Science & Intelligent Systems at Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology (SCET). 📌 Application Process Submit online application form Upload academic documents Pay application/registration fee Attend screening/interaction (if applicable) Admission is based on: Academic merit in qualifying degree Eligibility criteria fulfillment Seat availability Step 2: Registration for International Progression Pathway (Optional) Students interested in progressing to Master’s programs at: Wintec Wellington Institute of Technology must register separately with the International Relations / Global Pathways Cell. The International Office will: Counsel students regarding suitable Master’s programs Guide on credit transfer eligibility Assist with documentation & application submission Support visa processing guidance Step 3: Academic Performance Requirement For international progression, students must: Successfully complete the PG Diploma Maintain required academic performance Meet English language requirements (IELTS/PTE as prescribed by NZ institution) Fulfill admission criteria of the partner university Final admission to the NZ university is subject to: Acceptance by the host institution Credit recognition evaluation Seat availability If a student does not receive admission from t he selected NZ university, they may: Complete the PG Diploma at SCET and receive the diploma certificate Apply for the next intake Explore alternate partner options The PG Diploma admission is independent of NZ university admission. International progression is optional and subject to eligibility. Eligibility Eligibility for Admission at Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology (PG Diploma) • Direct Admission (No Work Experience Required) Candidates must have: B.Tech / BE in Computer Science Engineering (CSE) / Information Technology (IT) MCA M.Sc. Computer Science • With Minimum 2 Years Relevant Work Experience (IT/Software Domain) Candidates with the following qualifications are eligible: B.Tech / BE in Non-CSE branches B.Sc. Computer Science / IT BCA / BCS or equivalent Eligibility for Master’s Progression at New Zealand Institutions • Successful completion of the PG Diploma at Sahrdaya with 65% marks • English Language Proficiency: IELTS Academic 6.5 overall Tuition Fees And Costs Credit Transfer Program PG Diploma in Data Science and Intelligent Systems 6 Months Campus Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology Tuition Fee (Resident Indian) 1,20,000 1 Year Campus Wintec – Master of Applied Information Technology Tuition Fee (Resident Indian) Approx. NZD 26,300 for full 12-month programme Campus WelTec / Whitireia – Master of Information Technology Tuition Fee (Resident Indian) Approx. chunk_83	Back to Home About Faculty & Staff Programmes Curriculum Facilities Achievements Outcomes Activities & Sports Association Newsletter Placements Internships HOD Message About Computer Science & Engineering The Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology is committed to excellence in education, research, and service. We prepare students to become leaders and innovators in the rapidly evolving field of computer science and engineering. Established 2009 NBA Accredited 400+ Students 20+ Faculty Vision To be a center of excellence in computer science and engineering education, fostering innovation, research, and ethical leadership to address global technological challenges and contribute to societal development. Mission To provide quality education in computer science and engineering through innovative teaching methodologies, cutting-edge research, industry collaboration, and comprehensive skill development to produce competent professionals and entrepreneurs. Department Overview The Computer Science and Engineering department offers comprehensive undergraduate and postgraduate programs designed to meet the evolving needs of the technology industry. Our curriculum combines theoretical foundations with practical applications, ensuring students are well-prepared for successful careers in software development, research, and entrepreneurship. With state-of-the-art laboratories, experienced faculty, and strong industry partnerships, we provide an environment that encourages innovation, critical thinking, and professional growth. Our graduates have consistently demonstrated excellence in various fields including artificial intelligence, data science, cybersecurity, and software engineering. chunk_110	Examination Conduct Guidelines and Regulations CONDUCT OF EXAMINATIONS & EVALUATION 1 Distribution of Examination Materials The Examination Materials will be distributed from the Autonomous Examination Cell. 2 Invigilation Invigilators shall be responsible for the fair conduct of the examination inside the examination hall. After the commencement of the examination, allotted bar codes shall be pasted on the space provided in the answer booklet and attendance sheet. The candidate's attendance shall be recorded by obtaining signature in the attendance sheet provided which shall be countersigned by the invigilator. Invigilators shall ensure that all candidates have returned the answer scripts. The answer scripts shall be handed over to the examination control room within 15 minutes after the examination. In case a candidate is found to have indulged in malpractice, his/her answer script shall be separately handed over to the CoE. Instructions to the Invigilators are given in Annexure I. 3 Collection and Bundling of Answer Scripts The answer scripts from the examination halls shall be received at the examination control room after each examination. Answer scripts shall be bundled separately for each course and details such as the name of examination, date of examination, packet code, course code and course name, semester and the number of answer scripts shall be entered on the packing slip. The packed and sealed bundles shall be kept in the safe custody of the DyC(Ex) till the despatch to the evaluation camp. 4. Academic Discipline and Malpractices in Examinations Every student is required to observe discipline and decorous behaviour in the examination hall. Any act of indiscipline, misbehaviour and unfair practice in examinations shall be punishable according to the nature
chunk_111	of offense committed by the student. 5. Post Examination The major activities involved during this phase includes the collection and verification of answer scripts, distribution of answer scripts to the examiners, valuation of answer scripts by various levels of examiners, providing necessary instructions and monitoring the valuation process, tabulation of marks and return of the valued answer scripts to the camp for revaluation purpose. All the activities of this phase will be under the direct control of CoE and Dyc(Ev). 6. Appointment of Evaluators ●The CoE shall appoint evaluators from the panel of examiners nominated by the departments to evaluate the answer scripts. ●Evaluators shall have handled the course at least once. ●The number of evaluators appointed for each course shall be based on the number of candidates registered. ●There shall be three evaluators for the evaluation in a given term for a given course. ●Communication regarding the details of answer scripts allotted for evaluation shall be sent to evaluators in advance. 7 Valuation Process 1.Initial Evaluation by Two Evaluators: ●All answer scripts will be evaluated by two evaluators. 2.Variation in Marks (≤ 15% of Maximum Marks): ●If the variation in total marks scored between the two evaluations is less than or equal to 15% of the maximum possible marks, the average mark (calculated question-wise) will be considered in the final tabulation of results. 3.Variation in Marks (> 15% of Maximum Marks): ●If the variation exceeds 15% of the maximum possible marks, the answer script will be sent for a third evaluation by an evaluator appointed by the Controller of Examinations (CoE). ●Final Mark Calculation: The final marks will be the average of the two closest scores obtained from the three evaluations. This
chunk_112	calculation will be done question- wise. (*) 8. Post Evaluation Audit ●Post evaluation audit shall be conducted for checking transparency and fairness of the evaluation. ●10% of evaluated answer scripts selected at random shall be audited by the expert. ●Adherence of evaluation to scheme and answer key provided shall be verified. ●Consistency of evaluation among answer scripts for a particular course shall be verified. ●Observations regarding inconsistency in evaluation observed if any shall be reported to the CoE. 9 .Uploading Marks Marks shall be uploaded by the evaluators and a hard copy from the portal by the evaluator concerned shall be handed over to CoE. 10.Storage of Evaluated Answer Scripts ●The answer scripts after evaluation shall be bundled, properly recorded and kept in safe custody at the evaluation camp for easy retrieval for three months from the date of publication of results and subsequently stored in the strong room for four years. Answer scripts after four years of storage will be disposed of, as per the decisions of the Academic Council (AC). ●The DyC(Ev) shall hand over evaluated answer scripts for re-evaluation or other purposes on- demand within prescribed time limits. 11. Laboratory / Project / Dissertation / Viva Voce The scheduling of all Practical Examinations shall be provided by the Head of the concerned Departments to the CoE sufficiently in advance of the scheduled commencement of the examinations. Evaluations in respect of the Laboratory Courses, Seminars, Mini Projects, Projects, Viva Voce examinations, etc., shall be duly completed by internal/external examiners appointed by COE from the panel submitted by the respective HOD. The faculty in charge shall upload the final marks to the portal within five working days after the examinatio

chunk_148	Back to Home MOU's & Exchange Programme IIIC - Strategic Partnerships for Innovation Excellence IIIC has established strategic partnerships with leading industries, research institutions, and academic organizations to provide comprehensive learning opportunities, industry exposure, and professional development for innovation and entrepreneurship. Training & Development Comprehensive skill development and professional training programs Research & Innovation Collaborative R&D projects and academic research initiatives Industry Partnerships Strategic collaborations with leading organizations Internship Programs Hands-on experience and project opportunities for students Our Partners 32 active MOUs AI Kyro The AI Kyro Startup Studio is a bold, university-powered innovation engine where real-world founders team up with curated student talent and faculty mentors to bring AI-first MVPs to life — faster, leaner, and smarter. Date: 2025-12-15 Academic Year: 2025-2026 Focus: The purpose of this MOU is to formalize a strategic collaboration to establish an elite innovation program within the College's ecosystem, transforming the institution into a recognized Skill-First Institute while providing exceptional real-world learning opportunities for students and faculty. Scope: Foundation Phase: Skill development and team formation with industry exposure , Project Phase: Real startup project assignment with mentorship support , Delivery Phase: MVP development, demo presentation, and career advancement opportunities Benefits: AI Kyro Shall Provide: Program Development &Management, Project & Mentorship Services, Financial & Career Benefits, Platform & Technology. Hykon India Limited Hykon, Powering the Nation, an ISO 9001 company, is a pioneering presence in the field of power electronics
chunk_149	with varied interests in Medical transcription, Information Technology, Solar Energy and so forth. Mr. Christo George, a committed and dedicated technocrat, set it up in 1991. Date: 2024-07-05 Academic Year: 2024-2025 Focus: The focus of the MoU is to practically enhance the technical and professional capabilities of students and faculty by providing expert guidance from Hykon and exposing them to current industry practices. It aims to strengthen the research culture within the institution by promoting collaborative research, consultancy, and technology development initiatives. A key emphasis is on bridging academia and industry by enabling students and faculty to work on real-world problems, participate in industry-based projects, and gain insights from practical industrial environments. The MoU also prioritizes improving student employability through structured internships, extended on-the-job training programs, and the possibility of job placements based on performance and available opportunities. Additionally, the partnership fosters continuous knowledge exchange by organizing workshops, seminars, short-term courses, and conferences in areas of mutual interest, ensuring ongoing learning and professional growth. Scope: The scope of this MoU covers a wide range of collaborative activities between Hykon and Sahrdaya, including strengthening industry–academic interaction through joint efforts in academic learning, research, training, and professional development. It extends to shared academic and research activities such as consultancy, product development, and technical projects, along with comprehensive skill development initiatives like internships, on-the-job training, expert lectures, workshops, and other competency-enhancing programs. The partnership also involve s mutual access to laboratories, testing facilities, and institutional resources on a preferential basis. To ensure effective execution, the MoU establishes coordination cells and a Network Coordination Committee to monitor and manage all activities. The agreement is valid for three years, with provisions for extension, and includes essential legal elements such as confidentiality, financial responsibilities, indemnity, and termination procedures. Benefits: The MoU brings significant benefits to both Sahrdaya College and Hykon India Ltd., creating a mutually enriching partnership. For students and faculty at Sahrdaya, it provides enhanced learning opportunities through exposure to industry-grade projects, hands-on training, and real-world applications, along with access to advanced research and product development environments. It strengthens skill development by offering expert mentoring, practical experience, and opportunities for upskilling through workshops and seminars led by Hykon professionals. The agreement also improves career prospects by providing structured internships, on-the-job training, and the possibility of recruitment based on performance, while giving students preferential access to Hykon’s facilities for academic work. For Hykon, the MoU supports a robust R&D pipeline by enabling collaboration with academic researchers and student project teams, fostering new product ideas and technological solutions. It also helps identify talented students for potential employment and offers cost-effective access to Sahrdaya’s facilities and academic expertise. Both parties benefit through shared intellectual property, stronger industry–academic ties, enhanced innovation, and efficient sharing of responsibilities and costs related to research, monitoring, and coor
chunk_150	dination. View MOU Document → Kerala Veterinary and Animal Science University This Memorandum of Understanding establishes a collaborative framework between the two institutions to promote cooperation in research, academics, product development, validation, entrepreneurship, and innovation. The MoU aims to facilitate the sharing of knowledge, technical expertise, and resources to enhance mutual learning and development in science, engineering, medical, veterinary, and allied disciplines. Through joint programs, training opportunities, exchange of data, and organized academic events, both parties will work together to create impactful research outcomes, strengthen educational processes, and support innovation-driven activities. Date: 2025-09-29 Academic Year: 2024-2025 Focus: The primary focus of this MoU is to encourage collaboration in research and development, student innovation, and interdisciplinary knowledge sharing. It aims to support joint academic initiatives, exchange of expertise in engineering, medical, and veterinary technologies, and engagement in product development and validation. The MoU also focuses on fostering entrepreneurship, facilitating professional training programs, and supporting innovation ecosystems through cooperation with Technology Business Incubators and startup activities. Scope: The scope of this MoU includes joint research activities, exchange of publications and data, and collaborative development programs across engineering, biotechnology, veterinary science, farm mechanization, and healthcare technologies. chunk_168	Overview Internal Quality Assurance Cell To keep up with the changes in higher education, educational Institution must actively engage themselves in the development and implementation of quality benchmarks in all their key performance areas. Committed to sustain its top ranking position, Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology has established the Internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) in 2015 to institutionalize the process of quality enhancement and sustenance in all the key areas of its delivery. The IQAC is acting as the nodal agency entrusted with the responsibility of ensuring total quality management by ushering in innovations leading to achieving excellence and adoption of intervention strategies for monitoring their successful implementation. Ever in quest for excellence, the IQAC functions to assure institutional accountability with probity to all the stakeholders, funding agencies in particular and society as a whole. Since quality enhancement is a continuous process, the IQAC has become a part of the institution’s system and work towards realization of the goals of quality enhancement and sustenance. The prime task of the IQAC is to develop a system for conscious, consistent and catalytic improvement in the overall performance; which would channelize all efforts and measures of the institution towards promoting its holistic academic excellence. Since quality enhancement is a continuous process, the IQAC has become a part of the institution’s system and work towards realization of the goals of quality enhancement and sustenance. The prime task of the IQAC is to develop a system for conscious, consistent and catalytic improvement in the overall performance; which would channelize all efforts and measures of the institution towards promoting its holistic ac

chunk_175	Functions Key functions and responsibilities of IQAC IQAC Functions 1 Development and application of quality benchmarks/parameters for various academic and administrative activities of the institution 2 Facilitating the creation of a learner-centric environment conducive to quality education and faculty maturation to adopt the required knowledge and technology for participatory teaching and learning process 3 Arrangement for feedback response from students, parents and other stakeholders on quality-related institutional processes 4 Dissemination of information on various quality parameters of higher education 5 Organization of inter and intra institutional workshops, seminars on quality related themes and promotion of quality circles 6 Documentation of the various programmes/activities leading to quality improvement 7 Acting as a nodal agency of the Institution for coordinating quality-related activities, including adoption and dissemination of best practices 8 Development and maintenance of institutional database through MIS for the purpose of maintaining/enhancing the institutional quality 9 Development of Quality Culture in the institution 10 Preparation of the Annual Quality Assurance Report (AQAR) as per guidelines and parameters of NAAC, to be submitted to NAAC

chunk_182	Objectives Core objectives and goals of IQAC The prime objective of the IQAC is to develop a system for conscious, consistent & catalytic improvement in the overall performance of institution. Key Objectives To develop a system for conscious, consistent, and catalytic action to improve the academic and administrative performance of the institution. To promote measures for institutional functioning towards quality enhancement through internalization of quality culture and institutionalization of best practices.
chunk_183	Quality Initiatives Quality Initiatives The Institutional Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) enhances institutional quality through its supportive cells like ISO, RDC, IEDC, IIIC, and Placement & Training. IQAC conducts semester and annual planning meetings to align activities with the Strategic Action Plan 2030. It also organizes ISO management review meetings, ensuring adherence to ISO 9001:2015 QMS standards, where critical areas affecting quality are reviewed, feedback analyzed, and corrective actions implemented. Key initiatives include the Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development Cell (IEDC), which earned recognition under Kerala Startup Mission's Local Entrepreneurship Advancement Programme (LEAP) and partnered with NASSCOM Startups. The Research Innovation Programme for women startups, funded by Kerala Startup Mission with a ₹25 lakh budget, supported 7 women researchers with grants up to ₹5 lakh. Four innovations from the college received recognition at the IEDC Idea Fest 2023. The Research and Development Cell (RDC) achieved the granting of four patents and the publication of three patents. Additionally, the RDC contributed 30 conference papers and 12 publications. These efforts reflect the institution’s commitment to quality, innovation, and research, fostering a robust environment for academic and entrepreneurial growth. The IQAC ensures continuous improvement through structured planning and monitoring processes. Quality Policy Quality Policy data statement blah blah balahhhh blah blah balahhhh blah blah balahhhh

chunk_187	Vision & Mission Vision To make quality the defining element of higher education in India through a combination of self and external quality evaluation, promotion and sustenance initiatives. Mission To arrange for periodic assessment and accreditation of institutions of higher education or units thereof, or specific academic programmes or projects; To stimulate the academic environment for promotion of quality of teaching-learning and research in higher education institutions To encourage self-evaluation, accountability, autonomy and innovations in higher education To undertake quality-related research studies, consultancy and training programmes, and To collaborate with other stakeholders of higher education for quality evaluation, promotion and sustenance. chunk_190	About NDLI Club Learn about NDLI Club and its mission NDLI CLUB National Digital Library of India (NDLI) is a virtual repository of learning resources which is not just a repository with search/browse facilities but provides a host of services for the learner community. It is sponsored and mentored by Ministry of Education, Government of India, through its National Mission on Education through Information and Communication Technology (NMEICT). Filtered and federated searching is employed to facilitate focused searching so that learners can find the right resource with least effort and in minimum time. NDLI provides user group-specific services such as Examination Preparatory for School and College students and job aspirants. Services for Researchers and general learners are also provided. NDLI is designed to hold content of any language and provides interface support for 10 most widely used Indian languages. It is built to provide support for all academic levels including researchers and life-long learners, all disciplines, all popular forms of access devices and differently-abled learners. It is designed to enable people to learn and prepare from best practices from all over the world and to facilitate researchers to perform inter-linked exploration from multiple sources. It is developed, operated and maintained from Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur. NDLI MEMBERSHIP Dear Students, National Digital Library of India (NDLI) is a virtual repository of learning resources which is not just a repository with search/browse facilities but provides a host of services for the learner community. It is sponsored and mentored by Ministry of Education, Govt. of India. National Digital Library of India Club (NDLI Club) is an enabler of career progression for students, job seek
chunk_191	ers, researchers and learners. NDLI Clubs are set up in institutes and nodal bodies, and these Clubs conduct events to facilitate students to develop knowledge, skill and traits beyond regular curriculum which are essential for their progression in respective professional domain. The events may be either physical or virtual. Virtual events are web-based and are conducted through the NDLI Club portal. The events are organized at respective institute level or city/district/state/regional level or all-India level. I request all the students to enrol themselves as member of the NDLI Club. The link for registration is given below. Use your personal e-mail ID (gmail) and the Passkey given below. Modern curriculum covers programming, networks, cloud computing, and software engineering Affiliated to KTU (APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University) and approved by AICTE. chunk_249	Computer Science Engineering Message from Head of Department Computer Science Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department Dean Message HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments CSE HOD Message Computer Science & Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Message From the Head of Department Join us in our journey of excellence, innovation, and impact as we shape the future of technology together..e Dr. Manishankar S Head of Department Computer Science Engineering Qualifications Ph.D. Computer Science 15+ Years Experiencee Welcome to Computer Science Engineering Welcome to the Department of Computer Science Engineering at SAHRDAYA COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING AND TECHNOLOGY. The department of Computer Science Engineering started in 2002 with an intake of 60, the present being 240, and has made astounding progress in the last 15 years. We have strong undergraduate and postgraduate programs in computer science. The Department was formed with the primary objective of providing world class education in the field of Information Technology, while addressing the problems of today and tomorrow. Right from its inception, the department has been offering excellent infrastructural facilities with a variety of computing platforms to aspiring professional students to meet the burgeoning demands of the IT industry. The Department with qualified and experienced team of faculty members dedicates to reinforce effective teaching learning process in the ambiance of helpful environment well supported
chunk_250	and stuffed with curricular along with co-curricular activities. Time to time department is organizing student development program and hands-on workshop to overcome curriculum-industry gap. The Department is wide open to innovative ideas, methodologies to establish itself as the most hunted excellent learning center in this part of the state. Computer Science Engineering students are in high demand as far as industry recruiters are concerned. In recent years, Indian Software Industry has boomed due to rapid increase in globalization consequently required more software engineers. Fundamental goal of our department is to provide innovative and quality education with high standard to achieve academic excellence and provides platform for the students to achieve their career goals. Dr. Manishankar S Head of Department, Computer Science Engineering

chunk_252	Computer Science Engineering Program Outcomes (PO) Computer Science Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department Dean Message HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Program Outcomes Program Educational Objectives Program Specific Outcomes Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments CSE Program Outcomes Computer Science & Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Program Outcomes (PO) Engineering competencies and capabilities every graduate achieves NBA / NAAC Accredited On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 11 program outcomes. PO1 Engineering Knowledge: Apply knowledge of mathematics, natural science, computing, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization as specified in WK1 to WK4 respectively to develop to the solution of complex engineering problems PO2 Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions with consideration for sustainable development. (WK1 to WK4) PO3 Design/Development of Solutions: Design creative solutions for complex engineering problems and design/develop systems/components/processes to meet identified needs with consideration for the public health and safety, whole-life cost, net zero carbon, culture, society and environment as required. (WK5) PO4 Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Conduct investigations of complex engineering problems using research-based knowledge including design of experiments, modelling, analysis & interpretati
chunk_253	on of data to provide valid conclusions. (WK8). PO5 Engineering Tool Usage: Create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering & IT tools, including prediction and modelling recognizing their limitations to solve complex engineering problems. (WK2 and WK6) PO6 The Engineer and The World: Analyze and evaluate societal and environmental aspects while solving complex engineering problems for its impact on sustainability with reference to economy, health, safety, legal framework, culture and environment. (WK1, WK5, and WK7). PO7 Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics, human values, diversity and inclusion; adhere to national & international laws. (WK9) PO8 Individual and Collaborative Team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse/multi-disciplinary teams. PO9 Communication: Communicate effectively and inclusively within the engineering community and society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations considering cultural, language, and learning differences PO10 Project Management and Finance: Apply knowledge and understanding of engineering management principles and economic decision-making and apply these to one's own work, as a member and leader in a team, and to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments PO11 Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change. (WK8)
chunk_254	Computer Science Engineering Academic Programmes Computer Science Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department Dean Message HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments CSE Programmes Offered Computer Science & Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Programmes Offered Academic programmes offered by this department B. Tech – Computer Science and Engineering (Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning) Programme Overview The B. Tech in Computer Science and Engineering (Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning) is a future-focused undergraduate program designed to equip students with strong foundations in computing along with specialized expertise in AI and ML technologies. The program blends core computer science principles with advanced topics such as deep learning, data analytics, natural language processing, computer vision, Generative AI, Agentic AI and intelligent systems. With a strong emphasis on hands-on learning, industry relevance, and innovation, students gain the skills needed to build intelligent applications and solve real-world challenges across domains like healthcare, smart cities, finance, robotics, and automation. The curriculum integrates theory, laboratory practice, and project-based learning aligned with emerging industry needs. Students are trained using modern tools, frameworks, and cloud platforms, preparing them for careers in AI-driven industries, research, and entrepreneurship Programme Highlights Programme Duration 4-year full-time programme,
chunk_255	affiliated to APJAKTU Sanctioned Intake 60 seats via KEAM counselling B.Tech – Computer Science and Engineering Programme Overview The B.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) is a comprehensive undergraduate program designed to build strong foundations in computing, programming, and modern software systems. The Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology was established in 2002 and has over two decades of academic excellence in delivering quality technical education, research, and innovation. The department is supported by experienced faculty, modern laboratories, and a strong culture of industry collaboration and student-driven innovation. The program emphasizes core computer science principles while offering flexible academic pathways through integrated micro-specializations in Software Engineering, Cyber Security, Cloud and Mobile Application Development, Data Science, and Quantum Computing. This structure allows students to gain deep expertise in emerging domains while maintaining a strong base in fundamental computing concepts. The curriculum covers essential areas such as algorithms, data structures, operating systems, databases, computer networks, and distributed systems, combined with hands-on labs, industry-oriented projects, and innovation-driven learning. Through the micro-specialization model, students can tailor their academic journey based on career aspirations, enabling them to develop domain-specific competencies aligned with evolving global technology trends. The program blends theoretical rigor with practical exposure, preparing graduates for dynamic careers in industry, research, and entrepreneurship. Programme Highlights Programme Duration 4-year full-time programme, affiliated to APJ KTU.
chunk_256	Card Text Annual Intake 240 seats via KEAM counselling, divided across 4 batches (60 seats each). M.Tech Regular – Computer Science & Engineering Programme Overview The M.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering offers advanced training in cutting- edge technologies with focus on AI/ML, Data Science, Cyber-Physical Systems, Quantum Computing, and Cybersecurity. The programme equips students with the skills to build intelligent systems, analyze complex data, explore next-generation computational models, and design secure digital ecosystems. Through research-driven learning and industry- aligned coursework, the programme prepares graduates to excel in emerging technology domains Programme Highlights Total Duration 2 Years (4 Semesters) affiliated to APJAKTU Sanctioned Intake 06 Ph.D - Computer Science & Engineering Programme Overview The Ph.D. in Computer Science and Engineering is a premier research programme designed to cultivate scholars, innovators, and thought leaders capable of advancing the frontiers of computing and interdisciplinary technologies. With a strong emphasis on original research, the programme enables scholars to explore advanced areas such as Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science, Cyber-Physical Systems, Quantum Computing, Cybersecurity, Distributed Systems, Internet of Things, Cloud Computing, and Natural Language Processing The interdisciplinary nature of the programme allows candidates to collaborate across domains including electronics, biomedical engineering, smart infrastructure, and computational sciences, enabling impactful real-world contributions. ns Examination Placements IQAC Departments Computer Science Electronics & Communication Electrical & Electronics Biomedical Engineering Biotechnology Resources Library Autonomous Clubs & Activities Alumni (ETLab)

PEO 1 On successful completion of the Program, the Graduates are expected to: 1. To take up challenging careers in suitable corporate, business or educational sectors across the world, in multi-cultural working environment. PEO 2 To develop and design innovative and novel solutions to solve real life problems in the domain of computer science. PEO 3 To be responsible citizens with good team-work skills, competent leadership qualities and holistic values.

chunk_259	Computer Science Engineering Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs) Computer Science Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department Dean Message HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Program Outcomes Program Educational Objectives Program Specific Outcomes Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments CSE Program Specific Outcomes Computer Science & Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Program Specific Outcomes (PSO) Domain-specific competencies unique to this programme Specialization Outcomes On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 3 program specific outcomes. PSO 1 To mould students to understand, analyze and develop computer technologies in the areas such as algorithms, system software, multimedia, web design, big data analytics and networking for efficient design of computer-based systems of varying complexity. PSO 2 To enhance knowledge in the evolutionary changes in computing and apply standard practices and strategies in software project development using open-ended programming environments in order to deliver a quality product for business success and meet the future challenges. chunk_262	Computer Science Engineering Department Advisory Committee Computer Science Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department Dean Message HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Students Department Advisory Committee DQAC Board of Studies Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments CSE Department Advisory Committee Computer Science & Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology CSE Department Advisory Committee Industry-academia collaboration for curriculum enhancement We have constituted a Department Advisory Committee (DAC) for the Department of Computer Science to plan the activities for the upcoming semester and to review the department’s performance in the previous semester. The primary objective of the committee is to identify curricular gaps in the Computer Science program and to propose suitable add-on courses, value-added programs, and other academic initiatives to bridge these gaps. The committee consists of distinguished Alumni, Academicians, Industry Experts from the IT sector, PTWA members, the Head of the Department, the Program Coordinator, and Senior Faculty Members. chunk_265	Computer Science Engineering Board of Studies Computer Science Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department Dean Message HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Students Department Advisory Committee DQAC Board of Studies Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments CSE Board of Studies Computer Science & Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Board Of Studies (BOS) Academic governance and curriculum oversight The Board of Studies (BoS) of the Department of Computer Science and Engineering is a statutory academic body responsible for designing, reviewing, and continuously updating the curriculum to ensure academic excellence and industry relevance. Comprising senior academicians, industry professionals, and researchers, the BoS ensures that the syllabus reflects current technological advancements, research trends, and professional requirements. It plays a vital role in maintaining academic standards, integrating Outcome-Based Education (OBE) principles, and aligning programs with regulatory guidelines while preparing students to meet global career and higher education opportunities. Mission 1. Impart relevant technical knowledge, skills and attributes along with values and ethics. 2. Enhance creativity and quality in research through project based learning environment. 3. Mould Computer Science Engineering Professionals in synchronization with the dynamic industry requirements. 4. Inculcate essential leadership qualities coupled with commitment to the society. Association Objectives Academic Enrichment The association aims to promote excellence in computer science education by organizing seminars, workshops, tutorials, coding sessions, and expert lectures for B.Tech and M.Tech students Research and Innovation Promotion It encourages research, innovation, and interdisciplinary collaboration through project exhibitions, hackathons, paper presentations, and research discussion forums. Industry–Academia Interaction The association seeks to strengthen connections
chunk_300	with industry professionals, startups, and research organizations through guest lectures, internships, industrial visits, and collaborative programs. Skill Development It focuses on enhancing technical, analytical, and problem-solving skills in emerging areas such as artificial intelligence, data science, cybersecurity, cloud computing, and software engineering Student Collaboration and Mentorship The association fosters collaboration between B.Tech and M.Tech students by creating mentorship opportunities, peer learning platforms, and joint technical activities. Professional Development It prepares students for higher studies, research careers, entrepreneurship, and employment through career guidance, competitive examination support, and resume-building initiatives

chunk_302	Computer Science Engineering Innovative Teaching Learning Methods Computer Science Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department Dean Message HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments CSE Innovative TLM Computer Science & Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Innovative Teaching Learning Methods Creating an open, creative and trustworthy learning environment The innovative teaching-learning process is designed to create an open, creative, and trustworthy space for students to grow, take risks, and feel comfortable in their patterns of learning. Faculty initiate key actions to foster a more entrepreneurial and innovation-driven classroom experience. Our Approach We combine traditional pedagogy with modern digital tools to deliver outcomes-based education aligned with industry needs and KTU guidelines. Teaching Methods Employed Digital Learning Classroom Quiz Virtual Labs Video Lectures Low-Stakes Testing Concept Mapping Roleplay Modern Tool Usage Miniature Scale Models MOOCs Concept Tests Audio-Visual Aids Collaborative Learning Industry Mentorship Activity-Based Learning Buzz Groups Pair Interaction Alumni Interaction Case Study Mastery Learning One-To-One Communication Website-Based Learning TLM Documents & Resources Activity – Based Learning At the end of every topic, students are instructed to design innovative products in their activity book Download PDF Hands On - Training Hands-on training is a practical approach to learning that emphasizes direct experience and acti ve participation. Rather than relying solely on theoretical instruction, this method allows learners to engage with tools, equipment, or software in real-world or simulated environments. By doing the work themselves, participants are better able to understand concepts, develop critical skills, and retain knowledge more effectively. Hands-on training is especially valuable in technical and skill-based fields, where learning by doing leads to greater confidence, competency, and job readiness. Download PDF Video Lecturers YouTube Channel: Students are provided with video lectures for some courses that they can access while they are away from the Institute Video lectures by the faculty members of our department are available on the YouTube Channel. Download PDF Case Study Demonstration A Case Study Demonstration is a detailed presentation of a real or hypothetical situation to explain concepts, analyze problems, and propose solutions. Download PDF Virtual labs Virtual labs: To provide remote-access to simulation-based Labs in various disciplines of Science and Engineering Download PDF Roleplay Roleplay is a technique that allows students to explore realistic situations by interacting with other people in a managed way to develop experience and trial different strategies in a supported environment. Roleplay stimulates the imagination and enhances social development, encourages friendship through cooperation, listening, and turn-taking. This activity makes the students develop their knowledge on a specified topic. It solves a particular issue, which creates an opportunity for decision making in a given environment. Students are instructed to complete the task by role-playing by interacting with their peers and try to complete the task assigned to them in their specific role.
chunk_303	Download PDF Virtual teaching Google Classroom Creating a Google classroom using google app. Students are made to join as members of the Google classroom. Lecture materials, Assignments, Quiz questions are posted in the online classroom. Download PDF Designing innovative products: At the end of every topic, students are instructed to design innovative products in their activity book. Download PDF Digital Submissions etlab is a centralized and automated administration. It is a non-conventional method that supports collaborative learning, creating and delivering learning materials, generating reports, student notifications, etc. Digital submissions: Posting the teaching materials to facilitate blended learning Download PDF Mastery Learning Project-Based Learning Project-Based Learning focuses on developing critical thinking and problem-solving skills in the students. Its inquiry-based method of learning to solve the problems given as projects to the students is a style of active learning. Team activities and team projects help students to encounter their ideas to promote self-development and growth. Download PDF Classroom Quiz A Classroom Quiz is a short assessment conducted during or after a lesson to evaluate students’ understanding of the topic taught. Download PDF MOOCs NPTEL The main objective of the National Programme on Technology Enhanced Learning (NPTEL) is to enhance the quality of engineering and science education in the country by developing content for undergraduate and postgraduate curricula using video and web-based courses. These courses cover the syllabi prescribed by universities and approved by AICTE. Download PDF Digital Submissions etlab is a centralized and automated administration. With strong academic guidance, modern laboratories, and a practical, industry-oriented learning environment, the department equips students to design innovative electronic and communication solutions and prepares them for careers in telecommunications, embedded systems, automation, IoT, and advanced electronics. Affiliated to KTU (APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University) and approved by AICTE. Industry-aligned curriculum with hands-on learning and project-based pedagogy. Advanced research and innovation in core and emerging ECE domains. Strong industry engagement ensuring quality training and excellent placement outcomes.
