chunk_314	Electronics & Communication Engineering Message from Head of Department Electronics & Communication Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments ECE HOD Message Electronics & Communication Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Message From the Head of Department Be part of a culture of excellence and innovation, shaping ideas into impact for the future Dr. Ambily Francis Head of Department Electronics and Communication Engineering Qualifications Ph.D. Electronics and Communication Engineering 15+ Years Experience Welcome to Electronics and Communication Engineering The Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology is one of the pioneering departments of the institution, established in 2002. Over the years, the department has grown into a centre of academic excellence, offering B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering, M.Tech in Embedded Systems, and Ph.D. research programmes, catering to the evolving demands of industry and research. The department is supported by a team of highly qualified and dedicated faculty members who actively engage in teaching, research, consultancy, and professional development. Our academic environment is strengthened by well-equipped laboratories and advanced research facilities that provide students with hands-on exposure to emerging technologies. Notable among these are the C2S VLSI Laboratory granted under MeitY, Cyber-Physical Sy
chunk_315	stems (CPS) Laboratory in collaboration with IICT, Germany, and an IoT Laboratory funded by IIT Bombay. These specialized labs enable students to gain practical experience in VLSI design, embedded systems, IoT, and cyber-physical systems. To bridge academia and industry, the department maintains active Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) with reputed organizations including C-DAC, IIT Palakkad, Star Innovations, and Inker Robotics, along with several other industry and research partners. These collaborations facilitate joint research, training programs, internships, and student projects. Our students have consistently secured prestigious internships and project placements at leading institutions and organizations such as IIT Palakkad, CUSAT, DRDO, NIT Surathkal, C-DAC, Hycon, WACE Electronics, and Inker Robotics. Such opportunities provide valuable exposure to real-world engineering challenges and research culture, enhancing their professional competence. Innovation and research culture are strongly encouraged in the department. Students actively participate in national-level project competitions, hackathons, and technical events, winning awards and project grants regularly. Industrial training is an integral part of the curriculum, ensuring that graduates are industry-ready and globally competitive. With strong academic foundations, advanced infrastructure, industry collaborations, and a focus on research and innovation, the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering continues to nurture competent engineers and researchers who contribute meaningfully to technological advancement and nation-building. Dr. Ambily Francis Head of Department, Electronics and Communication Engineering SAHRDAYA Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology, Kodakara, Thrissur Distri ct, Kerala - 680684 Phone: +91-480-2720500 Email: info@sahrdaya.ac.in Quick Links About Us Admissions Examination Placements IQAC Departments Computer Science Electronics & Communication Electrical & Electronics Biomedical Engineering Biotechnology Resources Library Autonomous Clubs & Activities Alumni (ETLab)
chunk_316	Electronics & Communication Engineering Academic Programmes Electronics & Communication Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments ECE Programmes Offered Electronics & Communication Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Programmes Offered Academic programmes offered by this department B.Tech - Cyber Physical Systems Programme Overview B.Tech program in Cyber Physical Systems (CPS) is being offered from the current academic year with a sanctioned intake of 30 students. This autonomous program is designed to provide an interdisciplinary learning experience integrating electronics, computing, control systems, IoT, and artificial intelligence, with a strong focus on hands-on training, industry relevance, and real-world problem solving. Programme Highlights Programme Duration 4-year full-time programme, affiliated to APJAKTU Sanctioned Intake 30 B.Tech- Electronics and Communication Engineering Programme Overview The Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering (ECE) at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology was established in 2002 and has over 22 years of academic excellence. The department has a sanctioned intake of 60 students for the B.Tech program. It offers a unique blend of strong theoretical foundation, hands-on laboratory training, industry-oriented curriculum, and active research culture, supported by modern laboratories and experienced faculty. Programme Highlights Programme Duration 4-year full-time pro
chunk_317	gramme, affiliated to APJAKTU Sanctioned Intake 60 Integrated M.Tech - VLSI Programme Overview B.Tech–M.Tech Program in VLSI (Very Large Scale Integration) with a sanctioned intake of 60 students. This integrated program is designed to provide a seamless academic pathway combining undergraduate and postgraduate studies, enabling students to earn both B.Tech and M.Tech degrees upon successful completion. The curriculum emphasizes strong fundamentals, advanced VLSI design, semiconductor technologies, system-level integration, and extensive hands-on training using industry-standard EDA tools, preparing graduates for high-impact careers in the semiconductor industry, research, and advanced technology development. Programme Highlights Programme Duration 5-year full-time programme, affiliated to APJAKTU Sanctioned Intake 60 M.Tech - Embedded Systems Programme Overview M.Tech program in Embedded Systems since 2002. Currently, the program is being conducted as an autonomous batch, with the present cohort being the third autonomous batch. The program is designed to provide advanced knowledge and practical expertise in embedded hardware, software, IoT, and real-time systems, enabling students to excel in research, industry, and innovation-driven careers. Programme Highlights Programme Duration 2-year full-time programme, affiliated to APJAKTU Sanctioned Intake 6 M.Tech - VLSI Programme Overview Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering (ECE) is offering a specialized VLSI (Very Large Scale Integration) program under the autonomous batch, with a sanctioned intake of 12 students. The program is designed to provide in-depth theoretical knowledge and hands-on expertise in IC design, verification, semiconductor technologies, and EDA tools, preparing students for high-end
chunk_318	careers in the semiconductor industry, research, and advanced system design. Programme Highlights Programme Details 2-year full-time programme, affiliated to APJAKTU Sanctioned Intake 12 Ph.D Programme Overview Electronics and Allied Courses
chunk_319	Electronics & Communication Engineering Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs) Electronics & Communication Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Program Outcomes Program Educational Objectives Program Specific Outcomes Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments ECE Program Specific Outcomes Electronics & Communication Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Program Specific Outcomes (PSO) Domain-specific competencies unique to this programme Specialization Outcomes On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 3 program specific outcomes. PSO 1 Apply the fundamental knowledge of Electronics and Communication Engineering to design and implement various types of electronic systems in communication, signal processing and embedded systems domain. PSO 2 Follow and adapt to the dynamic trends in the field of Electronics Engineering. PSO 3 Apply electronic hardware and software tools to find the solutions for problems encountered by the society.
chunk_320	Electronics & Communication Engineering Program Educational Objectives (PEOs) Electronics & Communication Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Program Outcomes Program Educational Objectives Program Specific Outcomes Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments ECE Program Educational Objectives Electronics & Communication Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Program Educational Objectives (PEO) Long-range goals that graduates are expected to achieve Educational Objectives On successful completion of the programme, graduates are expected to meet the following 3 educational objectives within a few years of graduation. PEO 1 To demonstrate technical competency and leadership to become professional electronics engineers leading to a successful career. PEO 2 To adapt to current trends by engaging in lifelong learning. PEO 3 To demonstrate commitment towards sustainable development for the betterment of society.
chunk_321	ding design of experiments, modelling, analysis & interpretation of data to provide valid conclusions. (WK8). PO5 Engineering Tool Usage: Create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering & IT tools, including prediction and modelling recognizing their limitations to solve complex engineering problems. (WK2 and WK6) PO6 The Engineer and The World: Analyze and evaluate societal and environmental aspects while solving complex engineering problems for its impact on sustainability with reference to economy, health, safety, legal framework, culture and environment. (WK1, WK5, and WK7). PO7 Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics, human values, diversity and inclusion; adhere to national & international laws. (WK9) PO8 Individual and Collaborative Team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse/multi-disciplinary teams. PO9 Communication: Communicate effectively and inclusively within the engineering community and society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations considering cultural, language, and learning differences PO10 Project Management and Finance: Apply knowledge and understanding of engineering management principles and economic decision-making and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, and to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments. PO11 Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change. chunk_328	Electronics & Communication Engineering Board of Studies Electronics & Communication Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Students Department Advisory Committee DQAC Board of Studies Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments ECE Board of Studies Electronics & Communication Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Board Of Studies (BOS) Academic governance and curriculum oversight The Board of Studies (BoS) of the Electronics and Communication Engineering Department is constituted to design, review, and update the curriculum and academic framework in alignment with emerging technologies, industry requirements, and regulatory guidelines. The BoS ensures that the program outcomes, teaching–learning processes, and assessment methods maintain high standards of quality to prepare graduates for successful careers in electronics, communication systems, embedded technologies, research, and entrepreneurship. chunk_332	Electronics & Communication Engineering Department Laboratories Electronics & Communication Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Department Labs Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments ECE Department Labs Electronics & Communication Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology ECE Department Laboratories State-of-the-art computing facilities equipped with modern technology The ECE department maintains a suite of fully equipped laboratories designed to support hands-on learning, research, and innovation. Each lab is fitted with the latest hardware and software to meet the demands of modern engineering education. 8 Labs Wi-Fi 300+ Systems ECE Lab Facilities Lab Details SIGNAL PROCESSING AND COMPUTING LAB Description: The Signal Processing and Computing Laboratory provides students with practical exposure to the analysis and processing of real-world signals using computational tools and algorithmic approaches. The laboratory supports hands-on learning in areas such as digital signal processing, data analysis, and numerical computing, enabling students to model, analyze, and interpret signals from diverse applications. Emphasis is placed on algorithm development, performance evaluation, and visualization to strengthen problem-solving and analytical skills. Systems Features: Faculty Incharge :Dr. Jensha Haennah J H V L S I & LOGIC DESIGN LAB Description: The VLSI & Logic Design Laboratory offers students a strong practical foundation in digital system desig n and integrated circuit development. The laboratory enables hands-on learning in logic design, digital circuit analysis, and VLSI system modeling using modern simulation and design tools. It focuses on developing design thinking by guiding students from basic logic concepts to complex system-level architectures, while emphasizing correctness, optimization, and verification of digital designs through structured experimentation. Systems Features: Faculty Incharge :Ms. Chinchu Jose MICROCONTROLLER LAB Description: The Microcontroller Laboratory provides students with practical exposure to embedded system development using microcontroller-based platforms. The laboratory enables hands-on learning in programming, interfacing, and control of peripherals such as sensors, actuators, and communication modules. It emphasizes developing real-time applications by integrating hardware and software components in a structured learning environment. Systems Features: Faculty Incharge : Ms. Reshma P S C P S LAB Description: The Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) Laboratory provides Electronics and Communication Engineering students with hands-on exposure to the design, integration, and validation of intelligent systems that tightly couple computation, communication, and physical processes. The laboratory supports practical learning in areas such as embedded systems, Internet of Things, sensors and actuators, control systems, and real-time system integration using advanced development platforms and simulation tools. It emphasizes translating theoretical concepts into functional cyber-physical prototypes, system-level implementations, and real-world applications, while enabling experimentation, performance evaluation, and innovation aligned with national CPS and smart technology initiatives. S
chunk_333	ystems Features: Faculty Incharge :Dr. Jensha Haennah J H COMMUNICATION LAB Description: The Communication Laboratory provides students with practical exposure to the principles and techniques of analog and digital communication systems. The laboratory enables hands-on learning in signal transmission, modulation and demodulation techniques, noise analysis, and system performance evaluation. It emphasizes understanding real-world communication behavior through experimentation, measurement, and analysis using standard laboratory instruments and simulation tools. Systems Features: Faculty Incharge : Ms. Binet Rose Devassy PROJECT LAB Description: The Project Laboratory provides students with a dedicated environment to conceptualize, design, and implement engineering solutions through project-based learning. The laboratory supports individual and team projects across diverse domains, encouraging students to apply theoretical knowledge to real-world problems. It emphasizes system design, integration, testing, documentation, and presentation, while fostering independent thinking, creativity, and research orientation. Systems Features: Faculty Incharge :Ms. Anju Babu RESEARCH LAB & C2 S LAB Description: The Research and Chips to Startup (C2S) Laboratory provides Electronics and Communication Engineering students with hands-on exposure to research-driven experimentation and semiconductor system design. The laboratory supports practical learning in areas such as signal processing, embedded systems, communication technologies, and VLSI design, using advanced simulation and industry-standard EDA tools. It emphasizes translating theoretical concepts into functional research models, chip-level design modules, and prototype implementations, while enabling data analysis, system v
chunk_334	alidation, and innovation aligned with national semiconductor and research initiatives. Systems Features: Faculty Incharge :Ms. Vidyamol K ANALOG CIRCUITS AND SIMULATION LAB Description: The Analog Circuits and Simulation Laboratory equips students with hands-on experience in the design, analysis, and simulation of analog electronic circuits. The laboratory supports practical learning in amplifier circuits, oscillators, filters, and power supplies, combining theoretical understanding with circuit-level implementation. Emphasis is placed on circuit behavior, performance evaluation, and simulation-based validation. Systems Features: Faculty Incharge :Ms. Siji Joseph

Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (CDAC) India /2022 Collaboration for advanced computing education, research projects, expert training, and facility development on campus. Focus: Expert lectures, R&D collaboration, setting up advanced computing labs, industrial training, pre-placement training, faculty exchange, joint conferences, methodology workshops, and professional development programs. Scope: A one-day hands-on training on “VEGA Processors and Ecosystems – the first indigenous development board by CDAC” was conducted on 13-05-2022 for 2020–24 batch students and selected students from the 2019–23 batch. Benefits: Provides exposure to indigenous processor technology, enhances student technical competence, supports research collaborations, and boosts placement readiness through CDAC-supported training. Indian Institute of Technology, PKD(IITPKD) /2025 Indian Institute of Technology Palakkad is dedicated to excellence in education, advanced research, innovation, skill development, and impactful collaboration with industry and society. Focus: Excellence in education, cutting-edge research, innovation, skill development, and meaningful industry and societal collaboration. Scope: Collaboration in academic programs, research, innovation, and skill development initiatives. This may involve joint training programs,
chunk_345	faculty development, student engagement activities, curriculum support, workshops, seminars, and knowledge exchange aimed at capacity building and societal impact. Benefits: Enhance academic and professional competencies through shared expertise, strengthen faculty and student skill development, promote applied research and innovation, and foster meaningful industry and societal engagement. It will also support capacity building, knowledge exchange, and the creation of sustainable educational and training outcomes. Additional Skill Acquisition Programme (ASAP) /2018 Collaboration to deliver ASAP courses and train faculty as master trainers under the Advanced Skill Development Centre division. Focus: Running skill development courses, faculty training, and strengthening vocational and industry-oriented programs. Scope: Provides structured skill training for students and equips faculty with advanced teaching and training capabilities, expanding institutional capacity in delivering high-quality skill development programs. Benefits: Enhanced student employability through certified ASAP courses, improved faculty competency as master trainers, and better access to industry-aligned skill development opportunities. Agappe Diagnostics LTD /2018 Agappe Diagnostics Ltd engaged in the development, manufacturing, and distribution of high-quality diagnostic reagents, kits, instruments, and automation solutions. Focus: Focuses on innovation, research, and accessible healthcare solutions, supporting clinical laboratories and healthcare institutions in India and globally. Scope: The collaboration will focus on training, skill development, research support, industry exposure, and knowledge exchange in medical diagnostics and related technologies. Benefits: Enhance skill develop
chunk_346	ment, provide industry exposure, strengthen practical learning, support research initiatives, and improve employability through industry–academia engagement. SCTIMST-TIMED /2021 Collaboration for training, IPR awareness, research support, and innovation development. Focus: Awareness sessions, webinars, training programs, and workshops on IPR and technology transfer. Scope: Support for teachers, students, researchers, and startups through access to services, guidance, and innovation-related programs. Benefits: Expert guidance on patenting strategy, enhanced research opportunities, exposure to technology transfer, and capacity-building for startups and students. Part Matters Pvt Ltd /2022 Internship and development partnership for student training and laboratory support. Focus: Internship programs, student development, and laboratory setup support. Scope: Skill enhancement for 3rd- and 4th-year students through structured internships and hands-on training. Benefits: Practical industry exposure, professional development opportunities, and access to laboratory resources when required. Jobin and Jismi IT Service /2022 Training by relevant experts, project guidance, development activities, placement and internship opportunities. Focus: Relevant experts, project guidance, development activities, placement and internship opportunities. Scope: Technical problem-solving, project planning, software development, coding skills, database handling, version control, communication, teamwork, and industry-oriented professional skills. Benefits: Industry exposure, practical training, hands-on project experience, skill development, expert mentorship, improved employability, and enhanced placement opportunities. chunk_377	Electronics & Communication Engineering Innovative Teaching Learning Methods Electronics & Communication Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments ECE Innovative TLM Electronics & Communication Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Innovative Teaching Learning Methods Creating an open, creative and trustworthy learning environment The innovative teaching-learning process is designed to create an open, creative, and trustworthy space for students to grow, take risks, and feel comfortable in their patterns of learning. Faculty initiate key actions to foster a more entrepreneurial and innovation-driven classroom experience. Our Approach We combine traditional pedagogy with modern digital tools to deliver outcomes-based education aligned with industry needs and KTU guidelines. Teaching Methods Employed Digital Learning Classroom Quiz Virtual Labs Video Lectures Low-Stakes Testing Concept Mapping Roleplay Modern Tool Usage Miniature Scale Models MOOCs Concept Tests Audio-Visual Aids Collaborative Learning Industry Mentorship Activity-Based Learning Buzz Groups Pair Interaction Alumni Interaction Case Study Mastery Learning One-To-One Communication Website-Based Learning TLM Documents & Resources Interactive Classroom – Live Coding in Classroom (AI) The instructor writes and runs AI code live while explaining each step, and students observe the real-time output and logic. This hands-on approach keeps students engaged and helps them u
chunk_378	nderstand how AI concepts work in practice. ICT Supported Learning – AI Etlab Quiz Students use the AI Etlab platform to take online quizzes that assess their understanding of AI concepts. Instant feedback and automated evaluation help improve learning and engagement. Real-life example – Automatic Streetlight (CAM) An automatic streetlight uses a light sensor to detect darkness and sends the signal to a controller. The controller processes this data and automatically turns the streetlight ON at night and OFF during daylight, saving energy. ICT Supported Learning – Design Thinking (NPTEL Assignment) Students use the NPTEL online platform to learn design thinking concepts through videos and digital resources. Assignments and quizzes help them apply these ideas using ICT tools for effective problem solving. Collaborative Learning – Group Project: Traffic Light System with Two Modes Students work in teams to design a traffic light system that operates in normal and emergency modes. Through collaboration, they apply logic, timing control, and problem-solving skills to build and test the system. Interactive Classroom – Classroom Group Study Project Students work in small groups to discuss a problem, share ideas, and collaborate on a solution. This approach encourages active participation, teamwork, and deeper understanding of the topic. Real-life example – Burglar Alarm (CAM) A burglar alarm uses a sensor to detect motion or a door opening and sends this data to a controller. The controller analyzes the input and, if an intrusion is detected, activates an alarm or sends an alert to the owner. Efforts to keep students engaged – AWP Animation (Ionosphere) An animated visualization of the ionosphere helps students see how radio waves travel, reflect, and bend in real time. This
chunk_379	makes the concept more interactive and easier to understand compared to static diagrams.

chunk_389	Electrical & Electronics Engineering Department of Electrical & Electronics Engineering Electrical & Electronics Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department General Brief Vision & Mission HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments EEE General Brief Electrical & Electronics Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About the Department Electrical & Electronics Engineering is an exciting and dynamic field that deals with the technology of electricity, especially the design and applications of circuitry and equipment for power generation and distribution, machine control and communications. Electrical and Electronics Engineers enjoy a high demand service status in modern manufacturing and industrial processes. It imparts quality education in multifarious disciplines of Electrical, Electronics, Communication, Control Engineering, etc. along with generation, transmission, and distribution of electrical power and a set of subjects especially focusing on the performance and design of DC, AC, and Special Electric Machines. The course curriculum combined with practical, produce skillfully competent professionals to face modern technological challenges. The department encompasses a fine blend of renowned as well as young and dynamic faculties. The faculty takes active interest in research and development activities. They regularly organize workshops, training programs, and related activities for the benefit of the EEE students’ community. Sponsored research projects are also undertak en by them, contributing to the growth of recent research trends in the field of electrical and electronics engineering. The infrastructure and lab facilities are upgraded from time to time and provide a good practical learning and innovative environment for the students and researchers. Affiliated to KTU (APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University) and approved by AICTE. Strong industry partnerships with excellent placement records and median salary of INR 3.20 Lakh. chunk_391	Electrical & Electronics Engineering Message from Head of Department Electrical & Electronics Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments EEE HOD Message Electrical & Electronics Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Message From the Head of Department Join us in our journey of excellence, innovation, and impact as we shape the future of technology together Dr. Vijikala V Head of Department Electrical and Electronics Engineering Qualifications Ph.D. Electrical Engineering 20+ Years Experience Welcome to Electrical and Electronics Engineering Welcome to the Department of Electrical & Electronics Engineering at SAHRDAYA COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING AND TECHNOLOGY. The Department of Electrical & Electronics Engineering focuses on providing strong academic foundations and practical skills in key areas of electrical science and technology. Electrical & Electronics Engineering continues to play an important role in modern industries such as power generation and distribution, automation, electric mobility, and communication systems. Our curriculum combines theory with hands-on laboratory experience to help students understand and apply concepts in Power Systems, Electrical Machines, Power Electronics, Control Systems, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy. The laboratories and facilities are updated regularly to support effective learning and technical development. The department has a dedicated team of faculty members who are actively invol ved in teaching, research, and student development. Workshops, training programs, and industry-supported activities are organized to enhance student learning beyond the classroom and to keep pace with technological developments. We aim to create a supportive academic environment where students can develop technical knowledge, problem-solving skills, and professional values. The department remains committed to helping students build successful careers and contribute positively to the engineering field. Dr. Vijikala V Head of Department, Electrical and Electronics Engineering

PO1 Engineering Knowledge: Apply knowledge of mathematics, natural science, computing, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization as specified in WK1 to WK4 respectively to develop to the solution of complex engineering problems. PO2 Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change. (WK8) PO3 Design/Development of Solutions: Design creative solutions for complex engineering problems and design/develop systems/components/processes to meet identified needs with consideration for the public health and safety, whole-life cost, net zero carbon, culture, society and environment as required. PEO 1 To utilize broad knowledge of Electrical and Electronics Engineering as a foundation to design, develop, and implement innovative solutions for real-world challenges. PEO 2 To equip with the skills to adapt to the technological advancements by engaging in lifelong learning and collaboration with industry and academia. PEO 3 To evolve as socially responsible engineers with a strong ethical commitment towards environmental sustainability.

chunk_400	Electrical & Electronics Engineering DQAC Electrical & Electronics Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Students Department Advisory Committee DQAC Board of Studies Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments EEE DQAC Electrical & Electronics Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology DQAC Electrical And Electronics Engineering Department Quality Assurance Cell The Department Quality Assurance Cell (DQAC) of the Electrical and Electronics Engineering Department is responsible for monitoring, evaluating, and enhancing the overall quality of academic and administrative processes. The cell ensures effective implementation of outcome-based education, promotes continuous improvement in teaching–learning practices, and supports faculty development and research initiatives. It works to maintain consistency in academic standards, strengthen internal evaluation mechanisms, and foster a culture of quality, transparency, and accountability. The DQAC also coordinates feedback collection, analysis, and action planning to ensure student-centric learning and sustained academic excellence. Each lab is fitted with the latest hardware and software to meet the demands of modern engineering education. 7 Labs Wi-Fi 300+ Systems EEE Lab Facilities Lab Details E L E C T R I C A L M A C H I N E S L A B Description: It captures the essence of hands-on learning and experiments with DC and AC machines, which is the core purpose of the lab. Systems Features: S4 EEL202 Electrical Machines Lab I S5 EEL333 Electrical Machines Lab II E L E C T R I C A L W O R K S H O P Description: The workshop includes familiarization of various equipment, tools and techniques used in electrical laboratory and practice of different basic wiring, lighting circuits. Students are benefited in such a way that they get an idea about household wiring and metering systems and various tools. They are also made aware of various safety precautions, electrical shocks and preventions. Students get an idea of use of tools used by electricians, wiring regulations, types of cables and electric ac
chunk_407	cessories including switches, plug, circuit breakers, fuses etc., symbols for electrical wiring schematics e.g. switches, lamps, sockets etc. Systems Features: S1 and S2 24ESL006 Basic Electrical and Electronics Workshop D I G I T A L E L E C T R O N I C S L A B Description: The laboratory consists of a single room with an area of 1040 sq.ft. The Digital Electronics Lab in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering is well equipped with modern facilities for the under-graduate courses. Students are divided into small groups so as to give them proper exposure. Laying a strong foundation for young engineers, the laboratory makes the students understand the fundamentals of Digital Electronics. The students will have hands-on experience of Logic gates, Combinational and Sequential circuits. The major equipment include digital IC trainers, microprocessor/microcontroller trainer kits, stepper motor interfacing cards, ADC interfacing modules, matrix keyboard interface modules, LED matrix display module, regulated dual power supplies. Systems Features: S4 EEL204 Digital Electronics Lab POWER ELECTRONICS LAB Description: The Power Electronics Lab offers hands-on training in power electronic devices and systems, including component analysis and experiments on converters, inverters, choppers, and motor control. Systems Features: S6 POWER ELECTRONICS LAB EEL334 A N A L O G E L E C T R O N I C S Description: The laboratory consists of a single room with an area of 1040 sq.ft. The Analog Electronics Lab in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering is well equipped with facilities for the under-graduate courses and research oriented studies. In under-graduate courses, students are divided into small groups so as to give them proper exposure. Laying a
chunk_408	strong foundation for young engineers, the laboratory makes the students understand the fundamentals of Analog Electronics. The Major equipment includes regulated dual power supplies, cathode ray oscilloscope (CROs), and function generators. Systems Features: S3 EEL203 Analog Electronics Lab M E A S U R E M E N T S & I N S T R U M E N T A T I O N L A B Description: Provides hands-on training in calibrating energy meters, measuring electrical parameters, and using advanced instruments to apply theoretical knowledge practically. Systems Features: S3 EEL201 Circuits and Measurements Lab S6 EEL332 Power Systems Lab A D V A N C E D E L E C T R I C A L E N G I N E E R I N G L A B Description: The laboratory consists of a single room with an area of 1200 sq.ft. The Advanced Electrical Engineering Lab in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering is well equipped with modern facilities for the different courses and project oriented studies. Small groups of students are formed in order to provide them with appropriate exposure. Laying a strong foundation for young engineers, the laboratory makes the students understand the fundamentals of Control systems. They are given a strong base in Microprocessor, Microcontroller, and MATLAB keeping the objective of lab in mind. Advanced Electrical Engineering lab also includes popular system analysis software MiPower and Mathworks software MATLAB. Systems Features: CAPACITY : 30 SYSTEMS

chunk_414	Electrical & Electronics Engineering Memorandums of Understanding Electrical & Electronics Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements MoUs Faculty Achievements Faculty Publications Student Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments EEE MoUs Electrical & Electronics Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Memorandums of Understanding Strategic partnerships with industry and academic institutions The department has established formal Memorandums of Understanding with leading industry partners and academic institutions to foster collaborative research, student internships, faculty development, and knowledge exchange. 5 Total MoUs 5 Focus Areas 100% Active Status 5 Documented Partnership Agreements CADD Centre Training Services /2022 As Asia’s biggest network of CAD training centers, CADD Centre Training Services is the training arm of the 30-year-old CADD Centre Group, head quartered at Chennai, India. Founded in the year 1988, we have grown today as Asia’s biggest network of skill training provider in Computer Aided Design (CAD), Computer Aided Engineering (CAE), and Project Management. We host a wide array of courses, certifications, placement assistance and career planning tools to help students get the most from their education. Under the guidance of renowned administrators, directors, and faculty, CADD Centre Training Services recently marked its 30 years of excellence. We have also proudly crossed milestones having over 1000+ franchise outlets acros s 30+ countries during this time span. Focus: The focus of this MoU is to enhance technical competency of engineering students by providing training in professional CAD tools and industry-relevant design software from companies like Autodesk, PTC, ANSYS, Bentley and others. The agreement aims to support practical learning, improve software skills required for modern engineering industries and integrate globally recognized design technologies into academic learning and project work at Sahrdaya engineering programs. Scope: The scope of this MoU between Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology and CADCENTER is to collaborate in the Autodesk Academic Adoption Partner (AAP) program through which CADCENTER, being an Authorized Training Centre and Authorized Academic Partner of Autodesk, will provide Autodesk product training and Autodesk certification to students and professionals of the institution. The MoU covers both on-site and off-site training by qualified trainers of CADCENTER, using professional design, CAD and engineering tools that are recognized internationally. Benefits: The benefits of this MoU include access to professional Autodesk tools, certified training, globally recognized software certifications, exposure to industrial CAD platforms, and skill development aligned with engineering industry requirements. Students receive training from an authorized Autodesk academic partner, which improves their employability, strengthens design knowledge, and gives added value for project work, internships and future career opportunities in engineering design fields. Download Agreement Waves Electronics Private Limited (WEPL) /2024 Waves Electronics Private Limited (WEPL) was founded in 1972 by Mr. C. P. Philipose and Mr. P. I. Chacko, alumni of National Institute
chunk_415	of Technology, Warangal, South India. Waves is today the market leader in the manufacture of Industrial Battery Chargers, Marine Control Systems, Navigation Light Indicator Panels, Generator Automation, and Automatic Power Factor Correction Control Panels. WEPL cater to the leaders in Indian industry and export to countries in Europe, South East Asia, Middle East, and Africa. We produce high quality, performance tested, and fully certified control systems that provide a stable operation over the complete life cycle. Focus: The primary focus of this MoU is to build a strong linkage between industry and academia by encouraging collaborative research, innovation, and skill development. It aims to enhance SCET’s academic and research capabilities through active participation in WEPL’s industrial processes, design, and development activities. The MoU focuses on bridging theoretical learning with practical exposure by facilitating industrial training, expert lectures, workshops, and curriculum support. It also concentrates on fostering joint product development, knowledge sharing, and providing real-world experience to students and faculty through sustained interaction with WEPL engineers and facilities. Scope: The scope of this MoU encompasses comprehensive collaboration between WEPL and SCET to strengthen industry–academia engagement through joint programs, research activities, and knowledge exchange. It includes sharing of facilities, expertise, and resources to enhance education, research, and industrial exposure. The scope further covers joint product development, simulation and design analysis, faculty training, curriculum development support, student internships, industrial training, workshops, and mentoring programs. A Coordination Cell will also be established at b oth institutions to monitor, implement, and manage all activities under this MoU. Benefits: This MoU provides significant benefits to both institutions. SCET students gain exposure to real industrial environments, develop practical engineering skills, and receive opportunities for internships, project guidance, and mentoring from experienced WEPL professionals. Faculty members benefit from industry-linked training, consultancy opportunities, and enhanced research collaboration. SCET as an institution gains improved academic quality, stronger research output, and greater industry relevance. WEPL benefits by accessing trained student talent, collaborative research support, and academic expertise that can contribute to product development and innovation. Both organizations benefit through mutual knowledge sharing, enhanced capabilities, and the creation of a long-term, impactful industry–academia partnership. Download Agreement Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (CDAC) /2022 Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (CDAC) is the premier R&D organization of the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) for carrying out R&D in IT, Electronics and associated areas. Different areas of C-DAC, had originated at different times, many of which came out as a result of identification of opportunities. Focus: The main focus of this MoU is to promote collaborative teaching, research and development in modern technological domains, and to encourage knowledge sharing between both institutions. It aims to support skill development, enhance academic quality, provide exposure to advanced technologies and develop technological solutions that contribute to socially productive outcomes. Scope: The scope of the MoU between Sahrdaya College of Engineering and
chunk_416	Technology and C-DAC includes collaboration in advanced technology areas such as Cyber Security, Cyber Law, Cloud Computing, 5G Networks, GIS, Power Electronics, Control Systems, VEGA processors and other emerging fields. It also covers academic cooperation through expert lectures, student projects, industrial training, joint conferences, workshops, faculty exchange, research programmes and other mutually agreed activities. Benefits: The benefits of this MoU include opportunities for students to receive industrial training, internships, pre-placement training and expert guidance in emerging technologies. It also enables joint research projects, funding possibilities, faculty development programmes, knowledge sharing and enhanced industry–institute interaction. Overall, the MoU strengthens research culture and improves the employability of students. Download Agreement Kerala Electrical & Allied Engineering Co. Ltd. (KEL) /2024 Established in 1964 in the State of Kerala, India, the Kerala Electrical & Allied Engineering Co. Ltd. (KEL) is a multifaceted company fully owned by the State government. Through it’s four production facilities, located in various districts of the State, this ISO 9001 : 2000 complaint company provides basic engineering services / products besides executing projects of national significance for high profile clients like the various defence establishments. The company manufactures and markets products like general purpose brushless alternators, brushless alternators for lighting and air-conditioning of rail coaches, medium power and distribution transformers as well as structural steel fabrications. Focus: The focus of this MoU is to create a strong and sustainable industry–academia interface by promoting collaborative research, knowledge exc
chunk_417	hange, and skill development between KEL and SCET. The partnership emphasizes the enhancement of research and product development capabilities through joint projects, simulation, design analysis, and consultancy activities. It also focuses on strengthening academic quality by involving industry experts in curriculum development, training programmes, and workshops. Additionally, the MoU prioritizes student growth by providing opportunities for internships, industrial training, mentoring, and exposure to real-world engineering practices. Overall, the focus is on fostering innovation, building technical competency, and enabling meaningful academic–industry integration. Scope: The scope of this MoU encompasses a comprehensive range of collaborative academic–industry initiatives aimed at enhancing education, research, and industrial exposure for both KEL and SCET. It includes the joint execution of industry–academic programmes such as research, consultancy, training, and development activities, along with the mutual exchange of facilities, expertise, and technical resources. The partnership also covers joint projects involving simulation, design optimisation, analysis, and product development, supported by faculty collaboration in curriculum development, knowledge sharing, and technical training. Student-focused initiatives—such as internships, industrial training, workshops, and structured mentoring—form a key part of the scope. To ensure smooth implementation and oversight of all activities, a Coordination Cell will be established at both institutions. Benefits: The MoU offers significant benefits to both KEL and SCET by enabling shared access to expertise, facilities, and collaborative opportunities. SCET benefits through enhanced academic quality, improved research capab
chunk_418	ilities, and increased student employability through internships, hands-on training, and mentorship provided by KEL professionals. Faculty members gain through technical training, exposure to industrial practices, and participation in joint research and consultancy projects. KEL benefits from access to academic insights, fresh talent, and innovative solutions emerging from collaborative research and student projects. Both institutions gain through mutual knowledge enhancement, shared intellectual property opportunities, and the establishment of a continuous pipeline of skill development, workshops, and conferences. Overall, the partnership strengthens innovation, productivity, and long-term academic–industry synergy. Download Agreement Hykon India Limited /2024 Hykon, Powering the Nation, an ISO 9001 company, is a pioneering presence in the field of power electronics with varied interests in Medical transcription, Information Technology, Solar Energy and so forth. Mr. Christo George, a committed and dedicated technocrat, set it up in 1991. Focus: The focus of the MoU is to practically enhance the technical and professional capabilities of students and faculty by providing expert guidance from Hykon and exposing them to current industry practices. It aims to strengthen the research culture within the institution by promoting collaborative research, consultancy, and technology development initiatives. A key emphasis is on bridging academia and industry by enabling students and faculty to work on real-world problems, participate in industry-based projects, and gain insights from practical industrial environments. The MoU also prioritizes improving student employability through structured internships, extended on-the-job training programs, and the possibility of job pl
chunk_419	acements based on performance and available opportunities. Additionally, the partnership fosters continuous knowledge exchange by organizing workshops, seminars, short-term courses, and conferences in areas of mutual interest, ensuring ongoing learning and professional growth. Scope: The scope of this MoU covers a wide range of collaborative activities between Hykon and Sahrdaya, including strengthening industry–academic interaction through joint efforts in academic learning, research, training, and professional development. It extends to shared academic and research activities such as consultancy, product development, and technical projects, along with comprehensive skill development initiatives like internships, on-the-job training, expert lectures, workshops, and other competency-enhancing programs. The partnership also involves mutual access to laboratories, testing facilities, and institutional resources on a preferential basis. To ensure effective execution, the MoU establishes coordination cells and a Network Coordination Committee to monitor and manage all activities. The agreement is valid for three years, with provisions for extension, and includes essential legal elements such as confidentiality, financial responsibilities, indemnity, and termination procedures. Benefits: The MoU brings significant benefits to both Sahrdaya College and Hykon India Ltd., creating a mutually enriching partnership. For students and faculty at Sahrdaya, it provides enhanced learning opportunities through exposure to industry-grade projects, hands-on training, and real-world applications, along with access to advanced research and product development environments. It strengthens skill development by offering expert mentoring, practical experience, and opportunities for upskilling
chunk_420	through workshops and seminars led by Hykon professionals. The agreement also improves career prospects by providing structured internships, on-the-job training, and the possibility of recruitment based on performance, while giving students preferential access to Hykon’s facilities for academic work. For Hykon, the MoU supports a robust R&D pipeline by enabling collaboration with academic researchers and student project teams, fostering new product ideas and technological solutions. It also helps identify talented students for potential employment and offers cost-effective access to Sahrdaya’s facilities and academic expertise. Both parties benefit through shared intellectual property, stronger industry–academic ties, enhanced innovation, and efficient sharing of responsibilities and costs related to research, monitoring, and coordination. chunk_428	Electrical & Electronics Engineering Electrical & Electronics Engineering Student Association Electrical & Electronics Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Department Association Events Organized Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments EEE Department Association Electrical & Electronics Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology ADROIT ELECTRICAL STUDENTS ASSOCIATION (AESON) The curtains of AESON was raised by Mr. Muralidharan (Deputy Chief Engineer, KSEB) on 17th September 2012. The AESON of Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology represents students within the Electrical and Electronics Engineering Department. Our goal is to organize social and professional activities which strengthen and enrich the community within the department, and to help publicize various research activities within the department. We organize social events, host talks by guest speakers and students, and help communicate with the department and the students. Vision “To create an environment where creativity, research, and technological advancement thrive, enabling students to become pioneers in electrical and related engineering fields.” Mission “To build a collaborative community that nurtures creativity, practical competence, and problem-solving abilities, while providing platforms for hands-on learning, research, and career development.” Association Objectives Knowledge Advancement Through EEE Programs To further the knowledge and awareness in the field of Electrical and Electronics
chunk_429	Engineering through conferences, workshops, seminars and awareness programs. Professional Guidance Sessions Inviting Guest lecturers of industrialists, academicians, management personalities to motivate and guide our members. Leadership & Professional Development To develop leadership and initiative, and to inculcate a sense of responsibility amongst the members of the Association Idea Exchange Forum To create a forum for the exchange of ideas between students and staff of the department and other departments in the school and provide a conducive atmosphere for social contact between the aforementioned. Industry Collaboration & Career Exposure To connect students with industry opportunities and career paths. Community Engagement & Service To promote social responsibility through outreach and service activities.

Faculty initiate key actions to foster a more entrepreneurial and innovation-driven classroom experience. Our Approach We combine traditional pedagogy with modern digital tools to deliver outcomes-based education aligned with industry needs and KTU guidelines. Download PDF ICT Enabled Teaching Learning Classrooms in the Department are provided with an LCD projector and a computer with LAN/smart board and high-speed internet access that allows for multimedia presentation. The faculty member can alternate between using either black board and the LCD projector or smart board judiciously allowing for a more efficient lecture delivery. Some classrooms in the department are provided with smartboards.Smartboard teaching modernizes the classroom by integrating technology to make learning more interactive, collaborative, and enjoyable. When used effectively, it supports diverse learning styles, encourages student participation, and helps bridge the gap between traditional and digital education. Faculty and students can also access the e-resources from the classroom through high speed wireless internet. The students can further access the server of the digital library from the comfort of their home with the help of the URL link provided in the college website. Central Library-E-resource e-books and e-journals can be accessed using the link given below. EBSCOhost: https://search.ebscohost.com IEEE Xplore: https://ieeexplore.ieee.org DELNET: www.delnet.in (http://www.delnet.in/) Dow
chunk_433	nload PDF Video Lectures Video lectures are a modern and innovative teaching–learning method that use audiovisual content to deliver concepts in an engaging and interactive way. They allow students to learn at their own pace, revisit difficult topics, and visualize complex ideas through animations, demonstrations, and real-life examples. Video lectures also support blended and flipped classroom models, making learning more flexible, accessible, and effective. Download PDF Virtual Teaching Learning Virtual teaching–learning refers to the use of digital platforms, online tools, and virtual classrooms to deliver education without the need for physical presence. It enables real-time interaction, multimedia-based learning, and flexible access to lessons from any location. This method supports personalized learning, collaboration through online activities, and continuous access to resources, making education more accessible and technology-driven. Download PDF Dissection Based Learning This method involves taking apart electrical machines and measurement instruments to examine their internal components, understand their construction, and explain their working principles. It is an active, hands-on approach that helps students connect theoretical knowledge with the physical structures and functionality of the equipment. Download PDF Assignment Based Teaching Learning Assignment-Based Teaching-Learning is an approach in education where assignments are used as a primary tool for facilitating learning. In this method, students are given specific tasks or projects to complete, which require them to apply what they have learned, conduct research, solve problems, and engage in critical thinking. This approach allows students to take responsibility for their own learning and encourages
chunk_434	them to deepen their understanding of topics beyond the classroom. Download PDF Workshops and Technical Talks Workshops and technical talks are interactive teaching–learning methods where experts share practical knowledge, emerging technologies, and industry trends with students. Workshops provide hands-on experience, skill development, and real-time problem solving. Technical talks expose learners to advanced concepts and real-world applications through expert insights. Together, they enhance practical understanding, encourage curiosity, and bridge the gap between classroom learning and industry practices. Download PDF Activity Based Teaching Learning It is a student-centered approach where learning is facilitated through hands-on activities, experiments, games, or real-world tasks. This method encourages active participation, problem-solving, and collaborative learning, making education more interactive and engaging. Download PDF Collaborative Teaching Learning Collaborative teaching–learning is an innovative TLM where students work together in groups to solve problems, share ideas, and construct knowledge collectively. This method encourages active participation, communication, teamwork, and critical thinking. By learning from peers and engaging in joint activities, students develop deeper understanding and social skills while teachers guide and facilitate the process rather than simply delivering information. Download PDF Low-Stakes Testing It refers to assessment methods that are designed to have minimal consequences for students grades or overall performance. Low-stakes tests focus on encouraging regular learning, self-assessment, and improvement, without the pressure of significant academic penalties. These tests aim to enhance learning by providing students wit h opportunities to demonstrate their understanding in a low-pressure environment. Online quizzes Classroom quizzes Download PDF

As the "mother of all engineering disciplines," Civil Engineering drives nation-building—from sustainable infrastructure and smart cities to tackling climate change, disaster resilience, and water management. Our graduates launch thriving careers worldwide as Structural, Geotechnical, Environmental, or Transportation Engineers, Project Managers, Urban Planners, and more, backed by a strong alumni network, internships, and faculty mentorship. Join us to blend creativity with engineering, build impactful careers, and transform society. Affiliated to KTU (APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University) and approved by AICTE. chunk_442	Civil Engineering Message from Head of Department Civil Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments CE HOD Message Civil Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Message From the Head of Department Dr Drisya M Head of Department CE Department Qualifications Ph.D. Civil Engineering Welcome to CE Department The Department of Civil Engineering is one of the youngest Departments in Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology. From its inception in 2012, it is striving to excel in different spheres in order to help the students to excel both in curriculum and research. The department has a core group of highly motivated faculty members having industrial and teaching experience. The knowledge and ideas they have assimilated in various Civil Engineering disciplines are furnished to the students through the best academic practices. The department’s academic and research activities are enhanced by the extensive knowledge of faculty members with advanced degrees and trainings from prestigious institutions. Our alumni network includes notable figures who hold prominent positions in private and government sectors worldwide. Our department became one of the top choices among students due to increased interactions with academia and industry. We collaborate closely with a variety of government and corporate agencies to guarantee that our research and teaching efforts have a significant societal impact. Dr Drisya M Head of Department, CE Departm ent
chunk_443	Civil Engineering Academic Programmes Civil Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments CE Programmes Offered Civil Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Programmes Offered Academic programmes offered by this department B.Tech Lateral Entry– Civil Engineering Programme Overview B.Tech lateral entry in civil engineering allows diploma holders to join directly in the second year, shortening the standard four-year program to three years. Programme Highlights Intake 3 Nos B.Tech in Civil Engineering Programme Overview Department has established in the year 2012 to equip students with the knowledge and skills required to plan, design, construct, and maintain infrastructure such as buildings, roads, bridges, and water systems. It provides a strong foundation in structural engineering, geotechnical engineering, transportation, environmental, and construction management. Programme Highlights Programme Duration 4-year full-time programme, Affiliated to APJ KTU. Annual Intake 120 seats via KEAM counselling, divided across 4 batches (30 seats each). Career Outcomes Civil Engineer/ Structural Designer. Tremendous Opportunities in Government and Non-Government Organisations. M.Tech in Structural Engineering & Construction Management Programme Overview The M.Tech in Structural Engineering & Construction Management aims to take up advanced technical and managerial roles in the construction and infrastructure sector. The programme integrates str
chunk_444	uctural analysis and design with modern construction management practices, emphasising lab-integrated learning, interdisciplinary flexibility, and strong project and research orientation. chunk_445	Civil Engineering Program Outcomes (PO) Civil Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Program Outcomes Program Educational Objectives Program Specific Outcomes Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments CE Program Outcomes Civil Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Program Outcomes (PO) Engineering competencies and capabilities every graduate achieves NBA / NAAC Accredited On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 11 program outcomes. PO1 Engineering Knowledge: Apply knowledge of mathematics, natural science, computing, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization as specified in WK1 to WK4 respectively to develop to the solution of complex engineering problems. PO2 Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions with consideration for sustainable development. (WK1 to WK4) PO3 Design/Development of Solutions: Design creative solutions for complex engineering problems and design/develop systems/components/processes to meet identified needs with consideration for the public health and safety, whole-life cost, net zero carbon, culture, society and environment as required. (WK5) PO4 Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Conduct investigations of complex engineering problems using research-based knowledge including design of experiments, modelling, analysis & interpretation of data to provide valid conclusions. (WK8).

chunk_447	Civil Engineering Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs) Civil Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Program Outcomes Program Educational Objectives Program Specific Outcomes Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments CE Program Specific Outcomes Civil Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Program Specific Outcomes (PSO) Domain-specific competencies unique to this programme Specialization Outcomes On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 3 program specific outcomes. PSO 1 Apply acquired knowledge in technical areas to Civil Engineering problems, including but not limited to Structural, Geotechnical, Transportation, Geo-informatics, Environmental, and Water Resources Engineering. PSO 2 Design a system, component, or process to meet desired needs within realistic constraints such as economic, environmental, social, political, ethical, health and safety, manufacturability, and sustainability. PSO 3 Familiarise with various facets of construction and its planning like project scheduling, resource and material management, understand the impact of engineering solutions in a global, economic, environmental, and societal contexts. ng Biotechnology Resources Library Autonomous Clubs & Activities Alumni (ETLab)

PEO 1 Apply the acquired knowledge and skills in Civil Engineering to become successful and competent professions, capable of contributing effectively to the domain of civil Engineering and to cater to the needs of the society. PEO 2 Demonstrate leadership qualities in the execution and management of Civil Engineering projects, upholding professional ethics and sustainability concepts. PEO 3 Engage in lifelong learning with a quest to keep abreast with the latest developments in Civil Engineering practice. ge of Engineering & Technology. All rights reserved.

chunk_457	Civil Engineering Department Laboratories Civil Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Department Labs Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments CE Department Labs Civil Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology CE Department Laboratories State-of-the-art computing facilities equipped with modern technology The CE department maintains a suite of fully equipped laboratories designed to support hands-on learning, research, and innovation. Each lab is fitted with the latest hardware and software to meet the demands of modern engineering education. 8 Labs Wi-Fi 300+ Systems CE Lab Facilities Lab Details C E W O R K S H O P Description: 30 Systems Features: The primary purpose of the CE Workshop Lab is to familiarise students with common construction materials and basic site operations used in building and infrastructure works. It develops practical skills and safety awareness and an understanding of labour and workmanship requirements that are essential for effective supervision and execution on construction sites. F L U I D M E C H A N I C S L A B Description: 30 Systems Features: Fluid Mechanics Laboratory (FM Lab) is a core facility where students experimentally study the behaviour of fluids at rest and in motion and verify fundamental fluid mechanics principles used in civil engineering. G E O T E C H N I C A L E N G I N E E R I N G L A B O R A T O R Y Description: 30 Systems Features: A Geotechnical Engineering Laboratory is a specialised facility w
chunk_458	here the engineering behaviour of soil is studied through standard index and engineering tests. It supports the analysis and design of foundations earth structures and soil‑related problems in civil engineering. S U R V E Y I N G L A B O R A T O R Y Description: 30 Systems Features: A Surveying Laboratory is a practical facility where students learn how to measure distances angles and elevations for planning and design of civil engineering projects such as roads buildings and irrigation works. It links theoretical surveying principles with hands‑on field practice using both conventional and modern instruments. E N V I R O N M E N T A L E N G I N E E R I N G L A B O R A T O R Y Description: 30 Systems Features: The environmental engineering laboratory offers students hands-on exposure to EPA approved water and wastewater quality testing procedures as is normally done at a water and wastewater treatment plant M A T E R I A L S T E S T I N G L A B O R A T O R Y Description: 30 Systems Features: A Materials Testing (MT) Lab in civil engineering is a facility where the properties and quality of construction materials like cement concrete steel bricks and aggregates are evaluated under controlled conditions. It supports safe and economical structural design by ensuring that materials meet relevant standards and specifications. T R A N S P O R T A T I O N E N G I N E E R I N G L A B O R A T O R Y Description: 30 Systems Features: A Transportation Engineering (TE) Lab is a specialised facility where students study highway and pavement materials as well as basic traffic characteristics through experiments. It links transportation theory with practical testing and design used in road projects. C A D L A B O R A T A R Y Description: 45 Systems Features: A Civil Engineerin g CAD Lab is a specialised computing facility where students learn digital drafting modelling and basic analysis for civil engineering projects. It supports the transition from manual drawing to modern software‑based design workflows used in professional practice.

chunk_473	Civil Engineering Civil Engineering Student Association Civil Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Department Association Events Organized Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments CE Department Association Civil Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology PACE The Civil Engineering Association (CEA) is a dynamic student body formed under the Department of Civil Engineering with the aim of enhancing academic learning through professional interaction, technical exposure, and community engagement. The association acts as a bridge between classroom education and real-world engineering practices, nurturing leadership, teamwork, and innovation among students. Vision To nurture competent, innovative, and socially responsible civil engineers by fostering technical excellence, ethical values, and sustainable development through active student engagement and professional collaboration. Mission Promote Technical Excellence Encourage Innovation and Research Inculcate Social and Environmental Responsibility: Develop Leadership and Teamwork Association Objectives To promote interest and awareness in emerging areas of Civil Engineering This objective aims to keep students updated with the latest trends, technologies, and research developments in Civil Engineering. To organize seminars, workshops, expert talks, and industrial visits that complement academic learning provides opportunities for students to interact with professionals, academicians, and industry experts. To encourage stu
chunk_474	dents to participate in technical events, model-making competitions, and paper presentations This objective focuses on enhancing creativity, innovation, and communication skills among students To foster collaboration among students, faculty, and industry professionals for skill development and career growth The association acts as a bridge connecting students, faculty members, and the industry. To cultivate social responsibility by engaging in activities related to sustainable development and community service encourages students to participate in social outreach programs such as awareness drives on water conservation, waste management, and eco-friendly construction To enhance leadership, teamwork, and organizational skills among students provides a platform for students to plan, coordinate, and execute events and activities, helping them develop essential soft skills.
