chunk_476	Civil Engineering Innovative Teaching Learning Methods Civil Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments CE Innovative TLM Civil Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Innovative Teaching Learning Methods Creating an open, creative and trustworthy learning environment The innovative teaching-learning process is designed to create an open, creative, and trustworthy space for students to grow, take risks, and feel comfortable in their patterns of learning. Faculty initiate key actions to foster a more entrepreneurial and innovation-driven classroom experience. Our Approach We combine traditional pedagogy with modern digital tools to deliver outcomes-based education aligned with industry needs and KTU guidelines. Teaching Methods Employed Digital Learning Classroom Quiz Virtual Labs Video Lectures Low-Stakes Testing Concept Mapping Roleplay Modern Tool Usage Miniature Scale Models MOOCs Concept Tests Audio-Visual Aids Collaborative Learning Industry Mentorship Activity-Based Learning Buzz Groups Pair Interaction Alumni Interaction Case Study Mastery Learning One-To-One Communication Website-Based Learning TLM Documents & Resources (MOOC) NPTEL Coursera Major core courses in the curriculum are linked with massive open online courses Faculty members and students are motivated to take online courses from various eminent platforms like NPTEL and Coursera.NPTEL:The main objective of the National Programme on Technology Enhanced Learning (
chunk_477	NPTEL) is to enhance the quality of engineering and science education in the country by developing content for undergraduate and postgraduate curricula using video and web-based courses. These courses cover the syllabi prescribed by universities and approved by AICTE.NPTEL Local Chapter:Our college is having NPTEL Local Chapter: It is a partnership between the college and NPTEL. Many students and faculty members in the department enroll for courses and get certified after successfully completing the course.http://nptel.ac.in/LocalChapterCoursera:Many faculty members and students in the department have completed online courses based on their area of interest. These courses also help the students to advance their skills for career development.https://www.coursera.org/ Download PDF ACTIVITY BASED LEARNING Study group activity - Students are instructed to conduct different engaging activities during study group activities. This inculcates leadership, communication skills, presentation skills, and responsibility in students. Context: Learn from other people’s experiences and background knowledge. Gain perspective and point of view, which increases listening and interpersonal skill. Download PDF Field and site‑integrated TLM On‑site teaching sessions at nearby construction sites, bridges, WTPs, or highways with guided worksheets: identify elements, sketch load paths, list materials, and relate to IS codes studied in class Download PDF TECHNICAL QUIZES (ONLINE AND OFFLINE) They convert a one‑way lecture into active learning by forcing every student to think and respond within a short time window. Concept check quizzes: 2–5 MCQs or numerical concept questions at the start or end of class on the day’s topic (e.g., choice of foundation type, critical flow, degree of indeterminacy
chunk_478	). Application‑based scenario quizzes: short design or field scenarios (e.g., select suitable retaining wall, propose a traffic control measure) where students pick the most appropriate option and justify in one line. Peer‑instruction quizzes: students first answer individually (using cards or Google Forms), then discuss with neighbours and re‑answer; the shift in responses shows learning gain and stimulates civil‑engineering dialogue in class Download PDF ICT, software and simulation TLM Simulation‑based learning using open or campus‑licensed tools: STAAD for comparative analysis of different structural systems. AUTODESK Software for Sketching and 3D modelling. Download PDF

chunk_484	Biomedical Engineering Department of Biomedical Engineering Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department General Brief Vision & Mission HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BME General Brief Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About the Department Established in 2002, the Department of Biomedical Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology is one of the earliest and most distinguished institution in Kerala. The department offers both M.Tech and B.Tech programs in Biomedical Engineering, blending engineering, medicine, and life sciences to develop professionals capable of designing, testing, and managing advanced medical technologies that improve healthcare delivery. Affiliated to KTU (APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University) and approved by AICTE. Accredited by the National Board of Accreditation (NBA), the department is recognized for its academic rigor, outcome-based curriculum, and strong alignment with industry and regulatory standards. Our graduates are highly sought after for their interdisciplinary knowledge and hands-on expertise, shaped by collaborations with leading MedTech industries and exposure to global best practices. The department has been identified as an Emerging national Center of excellence, conducting flagship training programs on behalf of the Government of India (BIRAC-NBM) and the Government of Kerala (K-DISC, KMTC). Through these initiatives, the department contributes to strengthening India’s medical
chunk_485	device standards, testing ecosystem, and regulatory capacity building. The department hosts three Center of Excellence that anchor its teaching, research, and innovation ecosystem: GE-Sahrdaya Centre of Excellence for Healthcare Technologies – advancing healthcare innovation and technology transfer.  Sahrdaya-Agappe Innovation Hub – promoting industry-integrated learning and IVD innovation.  Centre for Medical Equipment Testing and Consultancy (CMET) – supporting device safety, performance evaluation, and ISO 13485-based quality system training. These centers serve as national reference points for medical standards, device testing, and in- vitro diagnostics, bridging academia, industry, and regulatory stakeholders. Department of Biomedical Engineering SCET, Kodakara Department at a Glance 12 Faculty Members 60 Student intake 90% Placement 100% Internship Why Choose Us? Industry-aligned curriculum with hands-on projects Experienced faculty with research expertise State-of-the-art labs and modern facilities Strong placement record and industry connections

chunk_487	Biomedical Engineering Message from Head of Department Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BME HOD Message Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Message From the Head of Department Join us in our journey of excellence, innovation, and impact as we shape the future of technology together Dr.Jis Paul Head of Department BME Department Qualifications Ph.D. Biomedical Engineering 20+Yrs Experience Product Design, Medical Devices and Standards, Human Factors Design, Medical Imaging Welcome to BME Department The Department of Biomedical Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology, established in 2002, has been a pioneer in offering the B.Tech programme in Biomedical Engineering, one among the very few in the state. The programme, truly interdisciplinary and innovation-driven, continues to evolve with advancements in healthcare technology, emerging as a nationally recognized hub for producing industry-ready biomedical engineers. Over the years, the department has established itself as a centre for funded research, consultancy, and industrial collaborations. The GE Healthcare Center of Excellence and the Innovation Hub by Agappe Diagnostics stand as milestones in our journey toward bridging academia and healthcare industries. In addition, BIRAC ISO 13485:2016 certified training programmes are being conducted at Sahrdaya as part of the BIRAC-funded project, aimed at strengthening medical device
chunk_488	innovation, quality systems, and regulatory compliance. Our students and faculty have consistently brought laurels through their innovations—winning national and international recognitions such as the IEEE Global Humanitarian Technology Award, Gandhian Young Technological Innovation Award, and even securing a U.S. patent. The department also successfully introduced “LUMOS”, a multipurpose decontamination device, contributing significantly during the COVID-19 pandemic. As we move into 2025 and beyond, the department is committed to advancing research, translational innovation, and global collaborations. We are expanding our partnerships with leading healthcare industries, strengthening our alumni network, and continuously updating our curriculum to align with the latest trends in AI in healthcare, biomedical data analytics, medical imaging, and medical device innovation. We warmly welcome aspiring engineers who wish to be part of the next generation of healthcare technologists. At Sahrdaya, you will gain the knowledge, skills, and values to innovate for a healthier tomorrow. Dr.Jis Paul Head of Department, BME Department
chunk_489	Biomedical Engineering Program Educational Objectives (PEOs) Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Program Outcomes Program Educational Objectives Program Specific Outcomes Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BME Program Educational Objectives Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Program Educational Objectives (PEO) Long-range goals that graduates are expected to achieve Educational Objectives On successful completion of the programme, graduates are expected to meet the following 4 educational objectives within a few years of graduation. PEO 1 Establish the graduates as creative professionals in design, manufacturing, research and testing of biomedical devices ensuring quality and patient-safety. PEO 2 Pursue advanced courses in biomedical engineering at par with evolving state-of-the-art technologies. PEO 3 Evolve as successful engineers, entrepreneurs and inspirational leaders in society with moral ethical values. PEO 4 Engage in continuous learning through professional development and collaborations to nurture future generations of biomedical professionals
chunk_490	Biomedical Engineering Program Outcomes (PO) Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Program Outcomes Program Educational Objectives Program Specific Outcomes Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BME Program Outcomes Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Program Outcomes (PO) Engineering competencies and capabilities every graduate achieves NBA / NAAC Accredited On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 8 program outcomes. PO1 Engineering Knowledge: Apply mathematics, science, and engineering fundamentals to solve complex problems. PO2 Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, research, and analyze engineering issues to arrive at substantiated conclusions. PO3 Design/ Development of Solutions: Design systems/components/processes meeting specifications with public health,safety, cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. PO4 Conduct investigations of complex problems: Use research methods (e.g., experiments, analysis) to derive valid conclusions. PO5 Engineering Tool Usage: Select and apply modern engineering and IT tools to complex engineering tasks, understanding their limitations. PO6 The Engineer and The World: Apply reasoning with contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal, and cultural issues in engineering practice PO7 Individual and Collaborative Team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse/multi-disciplinary teams. PO8 L ife Long Learning: Recognize the need for ongoing learning and possess the skills to sustain it in a changing technological world.
chunk_491	Biomedical Engineering Academic Programmes Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BME Programmes Offered Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Programmes Offered Academic programmes offered by this department B.Tech Biomedical Engineering Programme Overview Established in 2002, the B.Tech program in Biomedical Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology is one of the earliest and most distinguished undergraduate biomedical programs in Kerala. The program was launched with a vision to integrate engineering principles with medical and life sciences to develop competent professionals capable of advancing healthcare technology. Affiliated to APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University (KTU) and approved by All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), the program is accredited by National Board of Accreditation (NBA), ensuring adherence to outcome-based education and global quality standards. The B.Tech curriculum emphasizes: Strong foundations in engineering, medical sciences, and electronics Hands-on laboratory training and industry exposure Medical device design and development Regulatory standards and quality systems Internship and hospital-based clinical training Through collaborations with leading MedTech industries and government-supported initiatives such as BIRAC-NBM, K-DISC, and KMTC, students gain exposure to real-world challenges in medical device innovation, testing, and compl
chunk_492	iance. The department’s Centers of Excellence—including the GE-Sahrdaya Centre of Excellence, Sahrdaya-Agappe Innovation Hub, and CMET—provide students with opportunities in device testing, ISO 13485 quality systems, and in-vitro diagnostics innovation. Over the years, the B.Tech program has produced graduates who are highly sought after in medical device industries, healthcare technology companies, regulatory bodies, research institutions, and higher education worldwide. Programme Highlights Programme Duration 4-year full-time programme , Affiliated to APJAKTU Sanctioned Intake 60 M.Tech Biomedical Engineering Programme Overview Launched in 2025, the M.Tech program in Biomedical Engineering is designed to build advanced expertise in healthcare technology innovation, regulatory science, and translational research. The program responds to the growing national demand for highly skilled professionals in medical device development, testing, quality assurance, and healthcare technology management. Affiliated to APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University and approved by All India Council for Technical Education, the M.Tech program leverages the department’s recognition as an Emerging National Centre of Excellence. Programme Highlights Sanctioned Intake 6 PhD in Biomedical Engineering Programme Overview The Department of Biomedical Engineering proposes to initiate a Ph.D. Programme in Biomedical Engineering to advance high-impact research in healthcare technologies, medical device innovation, and translational biomedical sciences. Building upon its strong academic foundation since 2002 and the launch of the M.Tech programme in 2025, the department is well-positioned to foster doctoral-level research aligned with national healthcare priorities and global technological advancemen ts. The Ph.D. programme will initially be supported by two recognized research supervisors with expertise in interdisciplinary domains of biomedical engineering, enabling focused and high-quality research guidance.

chunk_501	Biomedical Engineering Board of Studies Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Students Department Advisory Committee DQAC Board of Studies Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BME Board of Studies Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Board Of Studies (BOS) Academic governance and curriculum oversight The Board of Studies (BoS) of the Biomedical Engineering Department is constituted to design, review and update the curriculum and academic framework in line with emerging technologies, industry requirements, and regulatory guidelines. chunk_502	Biomedical Engineering Department Laboratories Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Department Labs Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BME Department Labs Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology BME Department Laboratories State-of-the-art computing facilities equipped with modern technology The BME department maintains a suite of fully equipped laboratories designed to support hands-on learning, research, and innovation. Each lab is fitted with the latest hardware and software to meet the demands of modern engineering education. 6 Labs Wi-Fi 300+ Systems BME Lab Facilities Lab Details DIGITAL ELECTRONICS LAB Description: The Digital Electronics Laboratory in the third semester introduces Biomedical Engineering students to the fundamentals of digital logic, circuit design, and practical implementation techniques used in modern biomedical instruments. This lab enables students to build, test, and troubleshoot digital circuits that form the core of medical devices and embedded healthcare systems. Systems Features: Translating digital logic theory into functional hardware. MEDICAL EMBEDDED COMPUTING AND VIRTUAL INSTRUMENTATION LAB Description: The Medical Embedded Computing and Virtual Instrumentation Laboratory in the fifth semester provides Biomedical Engineering students with hands-on experience in MATLAB programming and virtual instrumentation tools used for modeling, analysis, and visualization of biomedical signals a nd systems. The laboratory emphasizes software-based implementation of medical data analysis and graphical interfaces Systems Features: Translating theoretical biomedical data processing concepts into working MATLAB/VI modules. ANALOG ELECTRONIC LAB Description: The Analog Electronics Laboratory in the third semester equips Biomedical Engineering students with the knowledge and hands-on skills to design, analyze and test fundamental analog circuits used in medical devices and instrumentation. This lab bridges theoretical concepts with real-world applications in healthcare technology. Systems Features: Circuit design for low-noise precision measurement systems. CLINICAL INSTRUMENTATION LAB Description: The Clinical Instrumentation Laboratory (BML333) is designed to familiarize students with the instrumentation, operation, and practical aspects of biomedical equipment such as ECG machines, patient monitors, electrosurgical units, colorimeters, ventilators, and infusion pumps. This lab provides students with hands-on exposure to designing, implementing, and troubleshooting electronic circuits used in medical devices, as well as testing and calibration procedures essential for clinical applications. By working directly with diagnostic, therapeutic, and monitoring instruments, students gain valuable practical knowledge that bridges theory with real-world biomedical engineering practices. Systems Features: Bridging theory with real-world applications in biomedical engineering. BIOMEDICAL SIGNAL & IMAGE PROCESSING LAB Description: The Biomedical Signal and Image Processing Laboratory in the seventh semester equips students with advanced skills in the acquisition, analysis, and interpretation of biomedical signals and medical images. This lab integrates concepts from d
chunk_503	igital signal processing, image analysis, and machine learning to solve real-world healthcare challenges. Systems Features: Developing algorithms for early diagnosis monitoring and decision support. ELECTRONICS WORKSHOP LAB Description: The Electronics Workshop Laboratory introduces first-semester students to the fundamentals of electronic components, tools, and practical skills essential for engineering practice. This hands-on laboratory helps students identify, test and use basic electronic components such as resistors, capacitors, diodes, transistors, LEDs and integrated circuits. Students learn the safe handling of instruments, soldering and desoldering techniques, wiring practices, breadboard prototyping, and basic circuit assembly. Systems Features: Through carefully designed exercises the lab cultivates: Familiarity with measurement instruments such as multimeters power supplies and oscilloscopes. Understanding of circuit diagrams and their physical implementation. Skills in fault diagnosis and basic repair of simple circuits. Teamwork safety awareness and documentation practices.

chunk_509	BV Detection Device using Electrochemical Lactate Sensor & pH Strips”, comprising Anjaly Joseph, Joel Varghese Shibu, Sneha P. S., and Bastin Antony, achieved a remarkable milestone by being selected for the Young Innovators Programme (YIP) at the College Level (CAT 2_4) and advancing to the District-Level round. Their solution presents a low-cost, rapid diagnostic platform integrating electrochemical lactate sensing with pH-based biochemical screening—an innovative approach aimed at enabling early and accessible detection of Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) in point-of-care settings. This recognition highlights the team's strong problem-solving ability and their commitment to developing impactful healthcare technologies. /2025 2025-26 Arts 2K26 EBSA /2026 2025-26 TIH-IoT, IIT Bombay -Grant-(NM-ICPS). Mr. Jibin Jose and Ms. Supriya Mary Sunil from the Biomedical Department have secured a major grant of ₹49.78 lakh from TIH-IoT, IIT Bombay, under the Government of India’s National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS). Their project, titled “Breast Cancer Screening Device using Transillumination and Near Infrared Spectroscopy,” focuses on developing a cost-effective, non-invasive screening technology aimed at enabling early detection of breast cancer. Supported under the Technology Development Project (TDP) scheme, this initiative underscores the team’s commitment to advancing accessible and impactful healthcare innovations. /2025 2025-26 Hawkins Roadies Event Mohamed Anzil, along with Jesvin Joju and Steve Gigi, participated in the Hawkins Roadies event at LUFTETAR 2026 organized by Sahrdaya College of Advanced Studies. chunk_511	Biomedical Engineering Biomedical Engineering Student Association Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Department Association Events Organized Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BME Department Association Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Engineering Biomedical Students Association (EBSA) The Engineering and Biomedical Students Association (EBSA) is the official student–faculty body of the Department of Biomedical Engineering. It serves as a collaborative platform that bridges students, faculty, and industry professionals to promote academic excellence, technical competence, and professional growth. EBSA organizes technical events, workshops, seminars, community outreach, and innovation challenges to nurture creativity and leadership among Biomedical Engineering students. Vision To empower students to become innovative, ethical, and socially responsible biomedical engineers capable of transforming healthcare through technology and research. Mission: Mission To foster a vibrant learning environment that integrates engineering principles with medical sciences. To enhance students’ technical, research, and professional skills through multidisciplinary exposure. To encourage innovation, teamwork, and entrepreneurship in the field of biomedical engineering. To establish strong connections between academia, healthcare institutions, and industry for collaborative learning and career development. Association Objectives To organize workshop s, technical talks, hands-on sessions, and industrial visits to bridge the gap between academics and the healthcare industry. To increase awareness about emerging technologies, ethical practices, and societal impact of biomedical engineering. To increase awareness about emerging technologies, ethical practices, and societal impact of biomedical engineering. To encourage leadership, teamwork, and soft-skill development through student-managed events. To foster entrepreneurship and innovation through incubation support, idea pitching, and start-up mentoring. To engage in social and community outreach programs related to healthcare and technology. To create a vibrant platform for biomedical engineering students to share ideas, innovations, and best practices. To promote awareness and understanding of emerging trends in biomedical engineering.
chunk_512	Biomedical Engineering Memorandums of Understanding Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements MoUs Faculty Achievements Faculty Publications Student Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BME MoUs Biomedical Engineering Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Memorandums of Understanding Strategic partnerships with industry and academic institutions The department has established formal Memorandums of Understanding with leading industry partners and academic institutions to foster collaborative research, student internships, faculty development, and knowledge exchange. 5 Total MoUs 5 Focus Areas 100% Active Status 0 Documented Partnership Agreements Kerala Veterinary and Animal Science University /2025 This Memorandum of Understanding establishes a collaborative framework between the two institutions to promote cooperation in research, academics, product development, validation, entrepreneurship, and innovation. The MoU aims to facilitate the sharing of knowledge, technical expertise, and resources to enhance mutual learning and development in science, engineering, medical, veterinary, and allied disciplines. Through joint programs, training opportunities, exchange of data, and organized academic events, both parties will work together to create impactful research outcomes, strengthen educational processes, and support innovation-driven activities. Focus: The primary focus of this MoU is to encourage collaboration in research and development, student
chunk_513	innovation, and interdisciplinary knowledge sharing. It aims to support joint academic initiatives, exchange of expertise in engineering, medical, and veterinary technologies, and engagement in product development and validation. The MoU also focuses on fostering entrepreneurship, facilitating professional training programs, and supporting innovation ecosystems through cooperation with Technology Business Incubators and startup activities. Scope: The scope of this MoU includes joint research activities, exchange of publications and data, and collaborative development programs across engineering, biotechnology, veterinary science, farm mechanization, and healthcare technologies. It also extends to supporting student project works, organizing seminars, workshops, conferences, and short-term programs of mutual interest. Additionally, the MoU covers training opportunities, shared access to expertise and facilities, participation in MOOC-based learning, and coordinated arrangements for stakeholder engagement, including startups and innovation centers. Specific collaborative projects will be defined under separate agreements as required. Benefits: This MoU provides extensive benefits for students, faculty, and research communities of both institutions. It enhances access to specialized technical expertise, research facilities, training programs, and domain knowledge across engineering, medical, and veterinary technologies. Students gain hands-on exposure to real-world projects, innovation practices, and entrepreneurship development opportunities. Faculty members receive platforms for collaborative research, joint publications, and professional networking. The partnership also strengthens institutional capability by enabling shared resources, promoting interdisciplinary resear ch, supporting startup ecosystems, and creating pathways for product development, validation, and funded innovation projects. Jubilee Mission Medical College and Research Institute /2016 This Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) establishes a collaborative framework between the two institutions to foster academic, research, and innovation-driven activities. The scope of the MoU includes promoting joint research, sharing expertise, facilitating skill development, and enabling the use of laboratories and testing facilities across both campuses. It also supports student training, project opportunities, and knowledge exchange for mutual growth. Focus: Academic collaboration, research promotion, innovation, and healthcare solution development. Scope: Student skill enhancement through shared laboratories, joint research, knowledge exchange, and hospital-based training. Benefits: Industry and hospital exposure, access to advanced testing facilities, improved research opportunities, and mutual academic enrichment. Meditix /2024 Technical Development Focus: Technical Development Scope: Student skill enhancement Benefits: Industry exposure Dr. Rani Menon MaxVision Eye Care /2025 This Memorandum of Understanding establishes a collaborative partnership between Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology and Dr. Rani Menon MaxVision Eye Care to advance academic research, technological innovation, and training in ophthalmology and biomedical engineering. The agreement provides a structured framework for joint research, internships, clinical exposure, medical device development, and community health initiatives. Through this collaboration, both institutions aim to strengthen their expertise, enhance student training, and promote advancements in ophthalmic technologies and
chunk_514	healthcare practices for mutual benefit. Focus: The focus of this MoU is to promote research-driven innovation in ophthalmic technologies, foster academic collaboration, and create opportunities for experiential learning in biomedical and clinical environments. It emphasizes joint research publications, patent development, hands-on exposure to ophthalmic diagnostic tools, and skill enhancement for both students and faculty. The collaboration also focuses on strengthening clinical knowledge, patient care understanding, regulatory compliance, and medical device validation processes. Scope: The scope of this collaboration includes establishing a research framework for ophthalmic technology development, undertaking joint research projects, and presenting research outcomes at national and international platforms. It also includes filing joint patents and supporting innovation in medical devices. The MoU covers structured training programs, internships, clinical workflow learning, exposure to ophthalmic diagnostic techniques, and faculty development initiatives such as workshops and technical seminars. Additional collaborative activities include the formation of an Ethics Committee, conducting medical camps, community health outreach programs, and organizing awareness initiatives such as free eye checkups and diagnostic screenings. Benefits: This partnership offers significant benefits including enhanced academic and research exposure, hands-on training in modern ophthalmic diagnostics, and real-time learning of clinical procedures. Students gain valuable industry-oriented skills, regulatory understanding, and practical experience that prepare them for careers in biomedical engineering and healthcare technology. Faculty members benefit from collaborative research opportuniti es, professional development programs, and access to advanced ophthalmic tools and technologies. The joint initiatives also contribute to community welfare through health camps, screenings, and awareness programs, creating a broad social impact while strengthening institutional capabilities. St. James Hospital Trust /2021 This MoU establishes a collaborative partnership between the two institutions to enhance academic learning, professional development, and community-oriented training. It focuses on knowledge exchange, joint research, skill development, staff collaboration, and providing students with real-world exposure through health education, workshops, field visits, and access to shared resources. Focus: The primary focus of this MoU is to strengthen public health education and awareness among students while promoting academic and teaching collaboration between the institutions. It aims to support joint research and training initiatives and foster professional behavior and community engagement. The MoU also emphasizes faculty–student exchange and mutual knowledge sharing to enhance academic and professional growth. Scope: The scope of this MoU includes conducting health education programs for students and facilitating the exchange of teaching materials, learning resources, and relevant academic literature. It further covers the organization of seminars, workshops, and conferences on mutually beneficial topics, along with collaboration on research and funded training programs. The agreement ensures students maintain ethical and professional behavior during community-based activities, provides reciprocal access to library facilities, and offers training and equipment support for student engagement. Additionally, the MoU outlines responsibilities for maintaining
chunk_515	activity records, supporting community field visits, and enabling faculty from both institutions to deliver invited talks. Benefits: This MoU provides significant benefits, including enhanced academic exposure and access to enriched learning resources. It opens avenues for research, project involvement, and participation in funded programs. Students gain practical skills through real-world training and community interactions, while both students and faculty benefit from broadened professional development opportunities. The MoU also offers access to additional equipment, laboratories, and library facilities, promotes stronger collaboration through academic events, and prepares students to become ethical, industry-ready professionals.

chunk_529	Biotechnology Vision & Mission Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department General Brief Vision & Mission HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BTE Vision & Mission Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Our Direction Vision & Mission Guiding principles that shape our commitment to academic excellence and innovation Vision Our Vision To be a national Centre of excellence in Bio Sciences and Bio Engineering Our Mission Educate the next generation of engineers with a relevant curriculum that will enable them to integrate biological sciences with engineering fundamentals that can create, model, and modify biological systems for industrial applicability. Encourage and facilitate research that would enable students to acquire new knowledge that is industrially and socially relevant. Train students to be leaders who value professional ethics and realize the need for societal contribution and environmental sustainability in their work Mission
chunk_530	Biotechnology Department of Biotechnology Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department General Brief Vision & Mission HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BTE General Brief Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About the Department The Department of Biotechnology was established in 2002 and has been steadily growing ever since. We offer a B.Tech program with an intake of 60 students each year. Initially following the KTU curriculum, the department transitioned to an autonomous system in 2024, giving us more flexibility to improve and update our academic programs. In addition to the undergraduate course, we also offer an M.Tech program in Industrial Biotechnology, which admits six students annually. The department is also offers Ph.D. programs in biotechnology. Our faculty members are dedicated to teaching, research, and student support. We focus on giving students a strong foundation in both theory and practical skills, preparing them for careers in research, industry, or further studies. The department aims to maintain a balanced, supportive learning environment where students can grow both academically and personally. Affiliated to KTU (APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University) and approved by AICTE. Strong industry partnerships with excellent placement records. Biotechnology SCET, Kodakara Department at a Glance 120 hgg 90% dd 4 4 Why Choose Us? Industry-aligned curriculum with hands-on projects Experienced faculty with research expertise State-of-the-art lab s and modern facilities Strong placement record and industry connections
chunk_531	Biotechnology Message from Head of Department Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BTE HOD Message Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Message From the Head of Department Join us in our journey of excellence, innovation, and impact as we shape the future of technology together Dr. Dhanya Gangadharan Head of Department BTE Department Qualifications Ph.D 13 Years Experience Welcome to BTE Department Over the past decade and more, the Department of Biotechnology at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology has established itself as a premier and most sought after college for pursuing Undergraduate Program (B Tech) in Biotechnology. A vibrant community of young, intellectual and talented citizens are moulded and nurtured here in the department. The department faculty is committed to undertake teaching and research as a passion with effective and fruitful methods of subject delivery towards etching the biotech engineers of tomorrow. The department is further building on the credible new outcome based methodologies of teaching and learning process to expand its departmental and institutional vision. Our department also addresses some complex issues pertaining to the area of human health and environment in society. An integral part of our endeavours has been our continuing science education and research towards new product and process development programmes. Commemorating the tenth year of the department and the colle ge; Masters Programme (M Tech) in Industrial Biotechnology was initiated in 2013. This platform of higher learning is embodied with the view upgrading the students to play significant role in the Biotechnology and Pharmaceutical industries globally. The department will continue on its course to achieve excellence in biotechnology towards the realization of its mandate in the field. Dr. Dhanya Gangadharan Head of Department, BTE Department

chunk_535	Biotechnology Program Outcomes (PO) Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Program Outcomes Program Educational Objectives Program Specific Outcomes Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BTE Program Outcomes Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Program Outcomes (PO) Engineering competencies and capabilities every graduate achieves NBA / NAAC Accredited On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 10 program outcomes. PO1 Engineering Knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems PO2 Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences. PO3 Design/ Development of Solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. PO4 Conduct investigations of complex problems: Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions PO5 Modern Tool Usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and mode rn engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities with an understanding of the limitations. PO6 The Engineer and Society: Apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the professional engineering practice. PO7 Environment and Sustainability: Understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development. PO8 Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice. PO9 Communication: Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large, such as, being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations, and give and receive clear instructions. PO10 Lifelong Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change.
chunk_536	Biotechnology Program Educational Objectives (PEOs) Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Program Outcomes Program Educational Objectives Program Specific Outcomes Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BTE Program Educational Objectives Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Program Educational Objectives (PEO) Long-range goals that graduates are expected to achieve Educational Objectives On successful completion of the programme, graduates are expected to meet the following 3 educational objectives within a few years of graduation. PEO 1 To be equipped with the engineering skills and technical concepts enabling them to take up careers in Biotechnology and allied fields globally. PEO 2 To instill the passion for lifelong learning scientific discovery, allowing them to conduct research, communicate and innovate technologies keeping them motivated to take research and higher education. PEO 3 To inculcate entrepreneurial and techno management skills along with professional and ethical responsibility empowering them to be responsible and socially aware citizens.

chunk_538	Biotechnology Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs) Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Program Outcomes Program Educational Objectives Program Specific Outcomes Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BTE Program Specific Outcomes Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Program Specific Outcomes (PSO) Domain-specific competencies unique to this programme Specialization Outcomes On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 3 program specific outcomes. PSO 1 To measure, model and manipulate properties of biological systems at the cellular and molecular level so as to produce sustainable products that address environmental and ethical standards PSO 2 To formulate and execute quantitative and design- oriented analysis of biological systems with modern tools and techniques that generate new knowledge at the interface of engineering and biology PSO 3 To transform as socially relevant biological engineers having a professional outlook that would enable them to work as a part of a team in an industrial, research or entrepreneurial set up and sustain a desire for higher learning and research.

chunk_548	Biotechnology Department Laboratories Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Department Labs Achievements Association Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BTE Department Labs Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology BTE Department Laboratories State-of-the-art computing facilities equipped with modern technology The BTE department maintains a suite of fully equipped laboratories designed to support hands-on learning, research, and innovation. Each lab is fitted with the latest hardware and software to meet the demands of modern engineering education. 4 Labs Wi-Fi 300+ Systems Lab Details MICRBIOLOGY LAB Description: The laboratory is well equipped to perform experiments on in vitro cultivation, quantification, preservation and characterization of microbes. The laboratory is also equipped with facilities for microbial analysis of food, water and soil. The microbiological experiments are performed in the laminar airflow chamber to maintain the biosafety. The lab also has facilities for sterilization and decontamination purpose. The laboratory also undertakes water and waste water testing as consultancy service which is approved by the Kerala State Pollution Control Board Systems Features: Equipped to perform experiments on in vitro cultivation quantification preservation and characterization of microbes BIOPROCESS ENGINEERING LAB Description: The lab is equipped with instruments to give a technical knowhow to students on microbial fermentation technology and downstream processin g of biomolecules to strengthen the knowledge of the design and operation of a bioreactor and basic techniques of microbial cell disruption, enzyme studies and protein purification. Systems Features: Equipped with instruments to give a technical knowhow to students on microbial fermentation technology MOLECULAR BIOLOGY LAB Description: The laboratory is well equipped for creating hands on experience on fundamental techniques of molecular biology and genetic engineering. The laboratory is also equipped to perform experiments in Biochemistry. The laboratory also has facilities for qualitative and quantitative analysis of biomolecules. Systems Features: Qualitative and quantitative analysis of biomolecules CHEMICAL ENGINEERING LAB Description: The department of biotechnology engineering offers students a unique learning experience with its Chemical engineering lab. The laboratory consists of a single room with 200 Sq.m area. An additional store room facility is also there to keep the consumables and glasswares which is attached to the lab. The laboratory is equipped with facilities to give students hands on experience on reaction engineering and process control and unit-operations experiments focused on mass and heat transfer and fluid flow measurements among other chemical processes. Systems Features: Single room with 200 Sq.m area.

Focus: Industry–academia interaction, student training and skill development Scope: Training by industry experts , joint product development and knowledge exchange, Internship and placement opportunities Benefits: Improved practical skills, employability and exposure to industry practices MoU with CARe KERALAM Ltd /2023 On 15 March 2023 the Department of Biotechnology signed MoU with CARe KERALAM Ltd (Confederation for Ayurvedic Renaissance-Keralam Limited). It provides facilities for the standardization of Ayurvedic medicines. The company has facilities including raw material centre, clinical trials, analytical laboratory, product ion centre etc. Focus: Ayurvedic medicine Scope: Skill enhancement Benefits: Industry exposure and practical training Vitaliz Bio science Pvt Ltd /2024 Aims to promote industry–academia collaboration by providing students with hands-on internship opportunities and offering faculty members specialized training aligned with industry practices and technological advancements. Focus: Industry-oriented student internships, faculty development through short-term professional training and exposure to practical tools, techniques, and real-world applications Scope: Internship programs for students lasting 4 to 6 months, tailored to VITALIZ’s operational needs and students’ academic requirements Benefits: Enhanced practical exposure and employability for students MoU with BiOZEEN /2023 Aimed at strengthening industry–academia collaboration by providing industrial biotechnology training to students and faculty, and by promoting joint research and development activities aligned with current industrial and research needs. Focus: Industrial training in biotechnology and exposure to industry-standard infrastructure and practices Scope: Collaborative research and development projects, including public- and industry-funded projects Benefits: Enhanced hands-on industrial exposure for students and Improved industry-readiness and skill development MoU with Arjuna Naturals Pvt Ltd /2023 Department of Biotechnology proudly takes its next step towards establishing industry academia collaboration. On 15 March 2023 MoU was signed with Arjuna Natural Pvt Ltd. It is India’s leading manufacturer and exporter of botanical extracts for pharmaceuticals and nutraceuticals. As a CSR initiative they have also sponsored a smart classroom for the department which was inaugurated on the sa me day. Focus: Training Scope: Skill enhancement Benefits: Industry exposure

chunk_561	Biotechnology Biotechnology Student Association Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Programmes Offered Outcomes & Objectives Curriculum & Syllabus Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Association Department Association Events Organized Innovative TLM Internships Department Activities Home Departments BTE Department Association Biotechnology Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology BEATS To foster academic excellence, innovation, and professional development among biotechnology students through collaborative learning, research initiatives, industry engagement, and holistic skill-building that prepares them to address global challenges in healthcare, agriculture, and environmental sustainability. Vision To be a national centre of excellence in Biosciences and Bioengineering Mission 1. Educate the next generation of engineers with a relevant curriculum that will enable them to integrate biological sciences with engineering fundamentals that can create, model and modify biological systems for industrial applicability. 2. Encourage and facilitate research that would enable students to acquire new knowledge that is industrially and socially relevant. 3. Train students to be leaders who value professional ethics and realize the need for societal contribution and environmental sustainability in their work. Association Objectives Academic Excellence To cultivate a culture of continuous learning and scholarly pursuit by providing students access to cutting-edge knowledge, expert guidance, and resources that enhance their understanding of biotechnology and related disciplines. Practical Innovation To bridge the gap betw een theoretical knowledge and practical application through hands-on workshops, laboratory sessions, projects, and collaborative experiments that encourage creative problem-solving and innovation. Research and Development To inspire and support student-led research initiatives, scientific investigations, and entrepreneurial ventures that contribute meaningful solutions to challenges in healthcare, agriculture, environmental sustainability, and industrial biotechnology. Industry Integration To establish strong connections with industry professionals, research institutions, and alumni through seminars, industrial visits, internships, and mentorship programs that enhance career readiness and professional networking. Collaborative Community To create an inclusive and supportive environment where students collaborate across disciplines, share knowledge, engage in peer learning, and build lasting relationships that foster personal and professional growth. Leadership and Skill Development To empower students with essential soft skills including communication, teamwork, project management, and leadership through event organization, technical competitions, and active participation in association activities.

Our Approach We combine traditional pedagogy with modern digital tools to deliver outcomes-based education aligned with industry needs and KTU guidelines. chunk_567	Applied Science & Humanities Department of Applied Science & Humanities Applied Science & Humanities Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department General Brief Vision & Mission HOD Message Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Innovative TLM Department Activities Home Departments AS&H General Brief Applied Science & Humanities Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About the Department The Department of Applied Science and Humanities (Formerly General Science Department) at Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology, established in 2002, stands as a beacon of excellence in technical education. Located in Kodakara, Thrissur, Kerala, our department is committed to providing world-class education and fostering innovation in the field of engineering. Affiliated to KTU (APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University) and approved by AICTE. Applied Science and Humanities SCET, Kodakara Department at a Glance 18 Faculty Count 5 Lab Staff Count 501 Student Count Why Choose Us? Industry-aligned curriculum with hands-on projects Experienced faculty with research expertise State-of-the-art labs and modern facilities Strong placement record and industry connections

chunk_569	Applied Science & Humanities Message from Head of Department Applied Science & Humanities Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Innovative TLM Department Activities Home Departments AS&H HOD Message Applied Science & Humanities Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Message From the Head of Department Dr. Sukhila Krishnan Head of Department ASH Department Qualifications Ph.D. Polymer Science Welcome to ASH Department Department of Applied Science & Humanities has access to all streams of students irrespective of their branch of specialization. The department helps them in moulding the right attitude towards Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Economics and English . It also imparts training in life skills such as communication skills, soft skills and personality development skills so that the students can face the challenges of the changing world with confidence. Each discipline the department undertake has its own lab with the latest equipment and software. The department is equipped with competent and enthusiastic faculty members ready to adapt themselves with the rapidly changing technical world . chunk_571	Applied Science & Humanities Program Outcomes (PO) Applied Science & Humanities Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Innovative TLM Department Activities Home Departments AS&H Program Outcomes Applied Science & Humanities Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Program Outcomes (PO) Engineering competencies and capabilities every graduate achieves NBA / NAAC Accredited On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 11 program outcomes. PO1 PO1: Engineering Knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems. PO2 PO2: Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences. PO3 3 PO3: Design/ Development of Solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. PO4 PO4: Conduct investigations of complex problems: Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions. PO5 PO5:Modern Tool Usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering a
chunk_572	ctivities with an understanding of the limitations. PO6 PO6: The Engineer and The World: Analyze and evaluate societal and environmental aspects while solving complex engineering problems for its impact on sustainability with reference to economy, health, safety, legal framework, culture and environment. (WK1, WK5, and WK7). PO7 PO11: Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change. (WK8) PO8 PO8: Individual and Collaborative Team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse/multi-disciplinary teams. PO9 PO9: Communication: Communicate effectively and inclusively within the engineering community and society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations considering cultural, language, and learning differences PO10 PO10: Project Management and Finance: Apply knowledge and understanding of engineering management principles and economic decision-making and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, and to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments. PO11 PO11: Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change. (WK8)

Systems Features: The Workshop Practice course makes students competent in handling practical work in engineering environment. PHYSICS LAB Description: Physics Lab at Sahrdaya College of Engineering has a with a wide range of equipments to cater to the needs of Dr A.P.J Abdul Kalam Technological University’s first year curriculum. It enables the students to have a hands on training on various topics they study in “Physics” theory course. The lab is designed spaciously and well-ventilated to accommodate a capacity of 30 students at a time. Systems Features: Here students were taught to find the wavelength of monochromatic light like sodium vapour lamp and polychromatic light like Mercury vapour lamp using spectr
chunk_586	ometer. Using travelling microscope and sodium lamp students were trained to measure the thickness of hair thin metal wire accurately. Students are trained to find the frequency of a tuning fork by Melde’s string making them vibrating it in longitudinal and transverse mode. CHEMISTRY LAB Description: Chemistry Lab at Sahrdaya College of Engineering has a with a wide range of equipments to cater to the needs of Dr A.P.J Abdul Kalam Technological University’s first year curriculum. It enables the students to have a hands on training on various topics they study in “Chemistry” theory course. The lab is designed spaciously and well-ventilated to accommodate a capacity of 30 students at a time. Systems Features: The laboratory’s design includes ceramic tiles on all working tables separate balance room and chemical store room.

Mission To provide a platform for students to enhance their technical skills, leadership abilities, and professional networks through workshops, seminars, competitions, and collaborative projects. Association Objectives Technical Excellence Promote cutting-edge technical knowledge and practical skills Industry Readiness Bridge the academia-industry gap through practical exposure Leadership Development Foster leadership and organisational skills in students Collaborative Learning Encourage peer learning and knowledge sharing Innovation & Research Support research initiatives and innovative projects Professional Network Build connections with industry professionals and alumni
chunk_600	Applied Science & Humanities Innovative Teaching Learning Methods Applied Science & Humanities Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology About Department HOD Message Newsletter People Faculty & Technical Staff Facilities Achievements Innovative TLM Department Activities Home Departments AS&H Innovative TLM Applied Science & Humanities Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology Innovative Teaching Learning Methods Creating an open, creative and trustworthy learning environment The innovative teaching-learning process is designed to create an open, creative, and trustworthy space for students to grow, take risks, and feel comfortable in their patterns of learning. Faculty initiate key actions to foster a more entrepreneurial and innovation-driven classroom experience. Our Approach We combine traditional pedagogy with modern digital tools to deliver outcomes-based education aligned with industry needs and KTU guidelines. Teaching Methods Employed Digital Learning Classroom Quiz Virtual Labs Video Lectures Low-Stakes Testing Concept Mapping Roleplay Modern Tool Usage Miniature Scale Models MOOCs Concept Tests Audio-Visual Aids Collaborative Learning Industry Mentorship Activity-Based Learning Buzz Groups Pair Interaction Alumni Interaction Case Study Mastery Learning One-To-One Communication Website-Based Learning TLM Documents & Resources Video Lecturers Students are provided with video lectures for some courses that they can access while they are away from the Institute Video lectures by the faculty members of our department are available on the YouTube Channel or via google classroom. Classroom Quiz A Classroom Quiz is a short assessment conducted during or after a lesson to evaluate studen ts’ understanding of the topic taught. This may be conducted via Etlab software or as a google quiz Virtual teaching Google Classroom Creating a Google classroom using google app. Students are made to join as members of the Google classroom. Lecture materials, Assignments, Quiz questions are posted in the online classroom. Mastery Learning Project-Based Learning Project-Based Learning focuses on developing critical thinking and problem-solving skills in the students. Its inquiry-based method of learning to solve the problems given as projects to the students is a style of active learning. Team activities and team projects help students to encounter their ideas to promote self-development and growth. Digital Submissions Etlab is a centralized and automated administration. It is a non-conventional method that supports collaborative learning, creating and delivering learning materials, generating reports, student notifications, etc. Digital submissions: Posting the teaching materials to facilitate blended learning

She has been actively involved in teaching, research, and mentoring, with a strong focus on nurturing innovation and practical problem-solving skills among engineering students. chunk_662	Back to Faculty Directory Dr. Vishnu Rajan Associate Professor Electronics And Communication Engineering Joined: 2011-01-01 AICTE ID: 01-441737121 vishnurajan@sahrdaya.ac.in Staff Room, Bio Block 22 Publications 27 Research 1 Awards 15 Years Exp. 1 Patents 4 Books Contact Information vishnurajan@sahrdaya.ac.in Staff Room, Bio Block Vaishnav, Edanad.p.o, Chengannur Areas of Interest No areas specified Memberships Ieee Biography Dr. Vishnu Rajan is an Associate Professor in the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology, Kerala, where he is dedicated to advancing teaching, technology, and innovation. He holds a PhD in Signal Processing and has strong interests in mobile technologies, embedded systems, and the applications of AI/ML in practical domains. In addition to his academic role at Sahrdaya, he is the Founder of BitSky, an MSME recognized by the Government of India under Udyam. Through BitSky, he develops and publishes Android applications on the Google Play Store, with more than 15 published apps, including 11 under the BitSky brand. His expertise lies in Kotlin and Jetpack Compose, and he continues to innovate in Android development. Dr. Rajan also contributes nationally as a Subject Matter Expert in Android App Development at Internshala and as a Google Certified Educator, delivering training programs and sessions across India. He has played a central role in organizing impactful events such as the Great Indian Hackathon (2024 & 2025), Google AI Campus Fest 2023, Google I/O Extended Thrissur 2022, and Android Campus Fest Thrissur 2022, which brought together industry leaders, Google Developer Experts, and students. At Sahrday a, he has been instrumental in hosting Faculty Development Programs (FDPs), symposiums, and workshops supported by AICTE, KTU, and KSCSTE. His efforts reflect a strong commitment to building a vibrant academic and developer community, while also strengthening Sahrdaya’s position as a hub for innovation and technology. In his spare time, Dr. Rajan enjoys working on Android development with Jetpack Compose, staying curious about emerging technologies and their potential to shape the future. S. Jyothi was born on July 18, 1964, in Nattika village, Thrissur district, Kerala. He completed his primary education at Central Upper Primary School, Nattika, and his secondary education at Government High School Valappad, Thrissur, both in Malayalam medium. He pursued his Pre-Degree education at Sree Narayana College, Nattika, and earned his B.Tech degree from Government Engineering College, Thrissur. In November 1988, Dr. Jyothi joined Malnad College of Engineering, Hassan, as a Lecturer in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering. He later obtained his postgraduate degree in Instrumentation and Control Engineering from REC Calicut (NIT Kozhikode) and his PhD in Electrical Engineering from Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore. Dr. Jyothi has published 19 papers in international journals and conferences and has attended about 65 workshops, training programs, and conferences. He presented a paper at an IEEE International Conference in Shanghai, China. He is a life member of ISTE, a Fellow of the Institute of Engineers (India), and a Senior Member of IEEE. Throughout his career at Malnad College of Engineering
chunk_671	, Dr. Jyothi has held various positions, including TEQIP Procurement Coordinator from 2013 to 2017, TEQIP Coordinator from 2017 to 2021 and Head of the EEE Department from 2021 to 2024. chunk_717	Back to Faculty Directory Dr. Asha Associate Professor Computer Science Engineering Joined: 2024-07-22 AICTE ID: 1-2194004978 drasha@sahrdaya.ac.in Decennial Block 6 Publications 3 Research 0 Awards 2 Years Exp. 1 Patents 0 Books Contact Information drasha@sahrdaya.ac.in Decennial Block Makkattil House, Kunnappily Po, Meloor, Chalakudy-680311 Areas of Interest No areas specified Memberships Iste Biography Dr. Asha S is an academician and researcher in the field of Computer Science and Engineering with a strong focus on Artificial Intelligence, Deep Learning, and Multimodal Data Analysis. She has contributed significantly to research in areas such as medical image analysis, deepfake detection, maritime surveillance using multi-sensor fusion, Vision Transformers, and multimodal emotion recognition. Her published work includes a Springer journal article in Multimedia Tools and Applications on deep learning-based skin cancer diagnosis integrating interpretability and advanced techniques. She has also developed curated datasets, including MMDFD for deepfake detection, and has worked on innovative architectures combining SAR imagery and AIS data for real-time maritime monitoring. In addition to her research contributions, Dr. Asha is actively involved in curriculum development, outcome-based education mapping, and mentoring undergraduate and postgraduate students. Academic and professional role He has extensive teaching experience in computer science, with interests spanning algorithms, data structures, programming in C, Python and Java, computer networks, logic design, and entrepreneurship. His professional philosophy centers on being an approachable mentor, fostering a positive learning environment, and continuously upskilling to guide students towards their full potential. chunk_772	Back to Faculty Directory Drisya M Associate Professor Civil Engineering Joined: 2021-08-30 drisya@sahrdaya.ac.in 3120 18 Publications 0 Research 2 Awards 5 Years Exp. 1 Patents 1 Books Contact Information drisya@sahrdaya.ac.in 3120 Kodakara Areas of Interest No areas specified Memberships Iste Ieee Biography Dr. Drisya M is an accomplished academician and researcher in Civil Engineering with over 14 years of teaching and research experience, specializing in Transportation Engineering. She currently serves as the Associate Professor and Head of the Department of Civil Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology, Thrissur. She earned her Ph.D. in Civil Engineering (Transportation Engineering) from the National Institute of Technology Calicut in 2021, following a Master’s degree in Urban Engineering from Anna University, Chennai, and a Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering from Calicut University. Her academic career includes teaching and research positions at renowned institutions such as the National Institute of Technology Calicut, Cambridge Institute of Technology Bangalore, and MVJ College of Engineering, Bangalore. She has also contributed to research projects in public transport optimization at Anna University, Chennai. Dr. Drisya has published widely in reputed journals and conferences, including Travel Behaviour and Society (Elsevier), European Transport, and the Transportation Research Board (TRB) Annual Meetings in Washington, D.C. Her research primarily focuses on urban traffic flow, level of service analysis, and simulation-based studies for congestion management. She has received notable recognitions, including the Best Paper Award at the 7th Conference on Transpo
chunk_773	rtation Systems Engineering and Management (2020) and the Best Teacher Award (2013). She has also contributed to departmental growth, leading her team in achieving NBA Accreditation for the Civil Engineering program at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology in 2022 and 2025. Beyond academics, she actively engages in professional service as a reviewer for international journals and conferences, and holds professional memberships in ISTE and IEEE. She has also been a key organizer of workshops, training programs, and faculty development initiatives in emerging areas of Civil Engineering, GIS, Road Safety, and Sustainable Infrastructure. Her innovative contributions extend to intellectual property, with a UK Design Patent (2023) granted for a Shallow Mounted Road Blocker. With a strong passion for advancing research, teaching, and industry collaboration, Dr. Drisya continues to inspire and mentor the next generation of Civil Engineers while contributing to advancements in transportation systems and sustainable urban development.
